description:
Zampple is a consumer club that operates in a physical location (zampple store) with a large number of carefully selected members who visit the store once every month and select a number of products they are interested in trying at home. After trial consumers log into their online account and complete a questionnaire. Brand managers can see results of both store take-out and usage at home along with category subcategory past initiative and demographic benchmarks in real time through a state of the art online platform. 

Freedom of choice (vs. traditional imposed placement) coupled with a "game" of earning points from evaluations dramatically improves quality and richness of feedback while at the same time product sampling and word of mouth initiatives build strong connections between brands and consumers. In this sense zampple provides premium sampling better quality product research and more efficient promotion through targeted Word of Mouth. Moreover zampple leverages demographic attitudinal habitual and other information in order to produce different kinds of targeted activities like Facebook posts coupons events and more.

name: Emotive Analytics
description:
Every marketing/consumer researcher is interested in knowing ‘why people do what they do;’ more specifically  ‘why people buy what they buy.’ Determining why is a huge issue and approaches to find out why people buy what they buy must incorporate a variety of components that make up what collectively can be called “decision dynamics.” The question of why becomes ‘what are the components of the decision-making process and how do they work together to direct a decision to buy?’ The components primarily include experiential stimuli (i.e.  anything that a person experiences in the world  both externally and internally as a result of sensory  physiological  perceptual  cognitive  and emotional processing) and the sensory  perceptual  cognitive  and emotional processing itself (of external and internal stimuli).

Of these components  recent research in neuroscience and psychology is showing that affective phenomena  of which emotions are one (and the word used most to collectively refer to most affective phenomena)  have been identified as the primary decision-making force. Yes  external and internal stimuli and non-emotional (i.e.  cognitive) processing are critical to the decision-making process; but when it comes down to it  emotions (and other affective phenomena) provide the value that directs sensory  perceptual  and cognitive processing (including “rationality”) to a conclusive decision. Jonathan Turner in ‘On the Origin of Human Emotions: A Sociological Inquiry into the Evolution of Human Affect’ may have said it best: “To select among alternatives requires some way to assess the relative value of these alternatives  and this ability to assess alternatives is tied to emotions. Emotions give each alternative a value and  thereby  provide a yardstick to judge and select among alternatives.”

Because of the predominance of emotions in the decision-making process  we refer to the decision-making dynamics as “emotional dynamics.” Returning to the issue of ‘why people buy what they buy ’ we see that the answer lies in revealing the emotional dynamics of consumer decisions and behavior.

But revealing the emotional dynamics of consumer behavior is challenged by two important characteristics of emotions (including emotional processing): (1) they operate largely unconsciously (or subconsciously  used synonymously here); that is  below our level of awareness and/or (2) they are often consciously and deliberately guarded because it is sometimes not socially desirable to express them or to admit that they are influencing our decisions and behavior. In other words  and collectively referring to these to characteristics as the implicit nature of emotions  people often can’t say or won’t say how they are feeling or how their feelings are affecting their decisions and behavior. This “can’t say  won’t say problem” challenges traditional research techniques that rely heavily on people explicitly (i.e.  conscious and deliberately) expressing their emotions. Therefore  implicit research techniques are needed to effectively reveal the emotional dynamics of consumer behavior  giving us the best indication of ‘why people do what they do.’

Emotive Analytics offers the following two-stage research procedure to effectively reveal the implicit and explicit emotional dynamics of consumer behavior  encompassing the key components of these dynamics (i.e.  external and internal stimuli and sensory  perceptual  cognitive  and  most importantly  emotional processing). The result of the two-stage research procedure is to discover the emotions (e.g.  pride  excitement  fear  happiness  love  etc.  both implicit and explicit) that are driving purchase and the experiences (external and internal) and cognitions (i.e.  thoughts  knowledge  beliefs  values  principles  self-images  etc.) that activate them.

name: Epitome Group
description:
Many have recognized that our 24/7 media environment has changed traditional approaches to brand positioning making brand storytelling increasingly relevant.  But how can brands systematically arrive at stories that are truly meaningful and make an unfailing connection with a target audience?  Could a technology be developed to reveal the meaning-making processes that motivate consumer behavior?  Discovering such a model was the challenge we set for ourselves over three years ago when we began developing NarrativeLogic™.

People live through stories and stories are thus the touchstone of human identity.  Brands are a universal feature of modern economic life and part of a system that produces commonalities.  These two simple truths are what make this technology work.  NarrativeLogic™ accesses the irrational mind--where the stories we tell ourselves live--and uncovers the drives and emotions that storytelling activates. We use proprietary interactive StoryScape techiques that aid us in what we call SubjectActivation. And the results have been striking.

In global quantitative testing concepts produced with NarrativeLogic™ consistently outperformed competitive concepts usually by a factor of two-to-one and frequently by three-to-one.  These early successes prove the power of narratology in understanding the contemporary human experience.

name: Forbes Consulting
description:
The Forbes Consulting Group was founded in 1985 to pursue the application of psychological theory and analysis to the solution of strategic business problems. Over the past 20 years  we have become a valued resource for Fortune 500 companies nationwide.

Our mission as a company is to offer our clients deeper  more profound levels of insight about needs and motivations – and to help them gain strategic market advantage on the strength of this insight.

Our service portfolio combines qualitative and quantitative research  consultation and ideation  letting us work as ongoing partners with our clients as they transform business visions into market realities.

A 96% client retention rate is perhaps the best testament to our success. Excellence at Forbes is not a goal  it's a standard.

name: Group Quality
description: GroupQuality® delivers an integrated self-service research toolbox  bringing a whole new level of accessibility  capability  affordability and opportunity when moving insight capturing online.

name: Gut Feeling Lab
description:
Funquête® (Fun + Enquete = Fun Questionnaires) is the research ASP for researchers and/or clients to communicate with consumers customers or employees.  With its multiple FQ door system researchers can repeatedly set and cast various FQ questions (like a gaming arcade) to registered respondents time to time during a certain period naturally deepening the insights overtime.

Funquete® simply follows respondent's natural cognition system: For example when asking “positioning” Funquête simply requests to position the ball on a map instead of asking it by complicated matrix questions.  Neither black boxes of neuromarketing nor rocket science statistics.  Because smartphone carries various sensor technologies (gyro motion touch etc.) that allow users to physically express their “gut feeling” we simply leverage them for insight research.  Funquête runs on smart devices thus scalable and easy to deploy.  Most of all it is fun and inspiring to both respondents and researchers (clients too).

Because it is fun Funquête needs less incentive.  Because it is fun Funquête is repeatable.  Because it is fun people respond naturally.  In the end respondents could become fan of the research industry or company.

Funquête is a new quality protocol between clients and consumers becoming a new media to interact.

name: Hynosis Focus Group
description: In  “awake” focus groups  you only get information from consumers' conscious level and miss the driving force behind their brand choices: their subconscious thoughts and feelings. Hypnosis Focus Groups is the only way to explore the subconscious level. Using "age regression" hypnosis takes respondents back in time to their earliest category and brand memories and the emotions connected to them. These memory "imprints" are the driving force behind their purchase motivations. (Konrad Lorenz won the Nobel Prize for his discovery of "imprinting" ). Once these "imprints" are known they can form the building blocks for more insightful brand strategies. There are also no "dominant" respondents who can sway the group. Every respondent (even the shyest) gives their own answer and opinion without being concerned about what any other respondent has said.  Respondents provide only truthful answers and will not tell us something just to make us feel good or make them look good: there is no "yea-saying." The information we get from them is coming from their subconscious (truthful) mind unfiltered by their conscious level. As a result we can rely on the information provided. Respondents will not hold back their emotions feelings intuitions and underlying beliefs because we have removed the respondents' need to be completely rational and logical. As a result respondents provide us with more than just "analytical" information. Respondents are also less inhibited in brainstorming exercises: they will come up with their most creative and wild and crazy ideas without worrying about how others will view them. As a result many more new and unconventional ideas will be developed.

name: Infosurv
description:
Infosurv’s prediction markets were developed to provide marketers and market researchers a more effective and efficient means of magnifying prime ideas and exposing greater insights during concept screening.  Our patented process taps into the “wisdom of crowds” by aggregating independent judgments of diverse participants into a collective prediction of future behavior of a specific target audience  without necessitating the inclusion of the target audience.  This application of crowdsourcing provides a true rank order for – and clear discrimination between – “winning” and “losing” concepts.  It also elicits qualitative comments that uncover concept strengths  weaknesses  and opportunities for improvement by probing on trading behavior and market selections.  In other words  participants tell us what to do to make each concept better  allowing best concepts to be improved prior to final concept testing.

In May of 2012  CNBC presented its “Squawk Box Blue Chip Book Award” to James Surowiecki for his book  Wisdom of Crowds.  The theories and stories in the book are brought to life by Infosurv’s prediction markets!

name: Market Research Group
description:

This creates a vicious cycle in relation to those businesses who have the budget to spend. SMBs make up 90 percent of the global workforce 65 percent of the world’s total GDP.

From a supplier perspective it’s innovative and different because the market potential (buyers) for market research firms increases massively. The suppliers are offered a risk-mitigated way of selling to this segment because only when a critical mass of buyers is reached the firm will actually do the research. Smaller market research firms can have a better chance to prove themselves against the ‘established order’. No other agency understands more about the way people experience brands.  For nearly a decade MESH has captured people’s experiences with brands in real-time. Our database of millions of experiences  collected over time  across country and category  reveals important trends and truths about the way people relate to brands today. Our unique ability to measure all forms of brand experiences and correlate that to brand performance is the single best way to unlock brand growth to date.MESH can help you unlock brand growth by uncovering the ‘black holes’ of knowledge  whether through consultancy or conducting a deep dive into the way people are experiencing yours and your competitor brands  using our award-winning Real-time Experience Tracking (“a new tool [that] radically improves marketing research”  Harvard Business Review  2012).  We’ll uncover a new perspective  create a state-of-the-art monitoring system for your essential brand metrics  and most importantly  help bring stakeholders along this transformative journey.Harvard Business Review  2012). name: Momentum Research
description:
There is so much to be learnt about respondents' feelings and thoughts by reading their facial expressions. Shouldn't facial coding be a core skill in qualitative research?

In testing TV ads it is well established - Unilever and Coca Cola are using facial coding to test all their ads in 2013. However  in testing press and poster ads  point of sale material and other types of communications  it is still an emerging technique.

The approach described here is based on human coding  rather than computer coding. Over the last three years we have demonstrated that facial coding by trained analysts is valid in identifying the most subtle of emotional responses. We have also proved that it has a high degree of reliability. In training analysts at major corporations and market research companies we have found that there is a high degree of agreement between coders  who can identify the same emotions to within 200 milliseconds.

With its power  simplicity and all-round applicability facial coding is set to become the leading
neuroscience-based method.

name: Office Reports
description: OfficeReports is a complete tabulation and charting solution for market research surveys that you use within Microsoft Office®. It lets you analyse  report and create presentations in a simple  unified way within the tools you are most familiar with.

name: OnCue
description:
OnCue® provides unprecedented flexibility in the selection of tools a customer can choose to deploy and conduct research with site visitors. This is made possible by OnCue’s foundational technology platform which helps customers gain control over their digital research or Voice-of-the-Customer strategy.  Similar to the way a content management system manages the content of a website more efficiently OnCue® adds a research layer to a website or mobile website that enables advanced sampling of site visitors and the integration of multiple research tools into the site’s research deployment. 

In addition OnCue® provides the tools to run and modify website research without the need to involve IT or introduce any coding.  It provides a central point to manage the research rules for all of the tools a customer chooses to deploy and enables various teams to run research at the same time while insuring the integrity of a visitor’s user experience. This guarantees that the same visitor does not get invited to multiple research initiatives in a single visit. OnCue® currently supports many popular self-service research tools and out of the box can support any research tool that uses a URL to get started.

name: Pocket Panda
description:
Our approach centers around the idea that smartphone adoption presents new opportunities to reach prospective respondents in new ways and that device capabilities and mobile-first thinking will open avenues for research to become more accessible relevant and accurate.

We're building a self-service research platform that connects researchers broadly defined with smartphone users engaged in using their favorite apps. To date mobile surveys have focused on being compatible with the smaller screens of mobile devices which doesn't solve the bigger problem of how and when to engage smartphone users to expand the reach to people who don't opt-in to traditional research panels.  The plethora of stand-alone survey-taking apps and their lack of traction is a great example of the lack of creativity in mobile market research today.

By starting with a focus on mobile and the belief that most if not all short form market research is better done on mobile we're unencumbered by the past to optimize survey creation the mobile surveys themselves and our analysis tools.  Users self select to complete our surveys that are integrated in their apps natively or via mobile web. Engaging respondents at the right moments when they are highly motivated with short easy to complete surveys increases the accuracy and completion rate.  An unsurprising 53% of our survey respondents said they typically quit surveys because they are too long dwarfing the 10% who said they won't complete because the questions are confusing.

name: Pollfish
description:
Pollfish has created the world's largest mobile consumer survey network  reaching over hundreds of millions of smartphone users to get data and insights.

Create a survey online in minutes  and get results in hours.

The Pollfish Survey Network enables you to do product concept testing  pricing research  measure your brand awareness  your marketing campaign’s impact  and gather consumer opinions quickly  easily  and much more cost-effectively - all in real-time.

name: RawData
description: RawData has an entirely new way to create market  media  and consumer research. Leaving traditional single media  single platform  handwritten diaries  telephone interviews  messy set-top box installations behind  RawData uses advanced technology to report exactly when and what media consumers were exposed to. Beyond strictly collecting quantitative research  RawData's technology platform enables the qualitative insight as well.
current_status: acquired by Market Tools - Chad is with Pure Spectrum

name: Social Decode
description:
We measure the engagement that television programs have  we analyze the comments made ​​by
users and we can translate them to understand customers  which can range from
TV stations  media agencies  enterprises and even content creators.
We can help in new ways not only for networks but also for brands  personalities and
government.
Our metrics include:
TOTAL TWEETS VOLUME: Total volume of social actions taken.
UNIQUE CONTRIBUTORS: Unique accounts taking an action related to a program.
ESTIMATED REACH: Approximate number of people impacted by a social action (sum of followers).
TWEET PEAK PER MINUTE: Maximum quantity of social actions per minute.
TIME SLOT UNIQUES: Unique accounts taking an action related to a program  during the transmission of that program.
TIME SLOT UNIVERSE: Accounts that commented about television during the transmission of a program.
TIME SLOT SHARE: (Percentage) Number of unique accounts that commented about a specific program during its transmission  divided by the universe of unique accounts
that commented about TV during that transmission time band.
SD RATING: (Points)
Number of accounts that commented during a show divided by the universe of
Twitter accounts that have commented about television. (As captured by SocialDecode 
the universe is currently growing overtime).

name: Standpoint Group
description: TeamBuilder is an efficient online screener that identifies the cognitive styles of qualitative study participants.  In product development qualitative research typically centers on understanding customer needs  idea generation  and building concepts.  There are cognitive styles that are ideally suited for each step in the process.  Our product trials indicate that the enhanced screening with TeamBuilder yields a more productive and engaged study participant.  A more productive team member improves the overall outcome of the investment in innovation research.

name: Tastemakers Research
description: Tastemakers Research Group has reinvented the way product testing is conducted. The “Pop-Up CLT” is at the intersection of traditional quantitative research  1:1 consumer engagement and sensory testing. This hybrid approach to product/taste-testing inherently contains significant cost  sample  sensory  speed  control and data advantages over current methods. Co-created by Andrea Poe and Carlos Barroso  the “Pop-Up CLT” is an important and exciting breakthrough in methodology innovation for consumer product testing.

name: Theseus DM
description: BrandAid: A plug and play brand relevance research tool  http://youtu.be/VzvV4j-EEX8  Product Optimizer:   A plug and play product feature sets research tool http://youtu.be/82qGtu2yEi0 for animated examples demonstrating the ease of use of each app.

name: Thrive Plan
description:
1. In their own homes  research respondents simply review a new product concept as it automatically plays across their computer screen.
2. Using respondents’ own home web cams  sophisticated software uses proven facial micro-expression algorithms to measure specific emotions each respondent is experiencing relative to every component of the concept.
3. Insights teams know  like never before  exactly how an idea makes people feel  with a much richer understanding of exactly WHY they're feeling that way.

name: Vatoca
description:
DGR leverages existing communities relationships and physical space to execute high-quality face-to-face research efficiently around the world in a cost-effective way. As an added benefit it does all of this while giving back to local communities non-profit groups and organizations.

Specifically DGR leverages an extensive  ever-growing network of nonprofits trade organizations schools  community groups and other organizations to recruit high-quality participants to share insights in a comfortable familiar environment. It is a completely new way to think about conducting face-to-face market research.

For consumer research we partner with relevant non-profits to attract high-quality fresh participants who are motivated by an opportunity to help their cause in a new and different way – specifically by sharing their time ideas and insights for market research. The non-profit gets paid for each participant they help recruit and also helps by providing physical space in which to conduct the research.

In B2B participants are recruited through their trusted trade associations and at trade events where they naturally congregate. They are drawn by the opportunity to influence future products for their industry while networking with peers.  It’s an extremely efficient way to reach high-quality difficult-to-recruit business people.

name: Xyte
description: Xyte  Inc.  a Madison  Wisconsin  privately owned  human behavior research company  is developing a website/app called Peopling™ for engaging millennials interested in finding compatible mates while becoming a test bed for advertisers to use in developing personalized targeted advertising.  Peopling™ will: (1) engage and match like-minded young people via website or on-the-go through a vibrating smart phone and (2) create a personalized mobile advertising system with statistically measureable results.  The app is free using Xyte’s scientific  brain based approach to find a more compatible soul mate and will spread quickly across campuses upon launch with events such as the “Amazing Race for Love”.  Personalizing means a single piece of ad inventory can be "split" into multiple tailored marketing messages based on the marketing preferences of clusters of Xyte’s cognofiles (cognitive profiles) at the mobile ad networks.  Each ad will be sent simultaneously via smartphones to the Peopling™ participants who are most likely to be engaged in the tailored message conveyed by both local and national advertisers.

name: Zappi
description:
We’ve changed the way companies access consumer insight by developing the most transformative  innovative solutions on the market.

Our mission? To provide an automated platform for buying cost-effective  world-class research services at speed - without trading off on quality. Which is why we partner with the world’s leading research agencies.

Zappi does away with the long-winded manual processes of traditional research - survey scripting  data collection  visualisation - and unlocks the capacity for cost-effective  high-quality testing at every stage of the creative journey.

name: ecGlobal
description: Enabling Smart Connections between Brands and Consumers

name: Ability Matrix
description:
The insight process is elevated to a new level by defining exact life situations the product or service is used in. We define the possible subjective associations to these situations. This helps define what emotional impact needs to be delivered during the use of the goods bought. Also it is cross demographic and cross cultural as the key emotional needs are the same only their respective share changes with each country.

With our method you can easily conduct brand-product-market fit analysis prior to prototyping.

name: Aperio Insights
description:
Consumers’ automotive purchase decisions are increasingly influenced by the in-car experience as they once were with exterior styling and raw performance. Developing the next generation of automotive apps and services depends on understanding the actual behavior of consumers in their cars. Asking consumers to self-report what they actually do use like and don’t like in their in-car infotainment experience is usually inaccurate unbalanced and often misleading. 

Traditional Methods Expensive & Ineffective — Increasingly complex in-car environment is also difficult to explore via traditional qualitative and quantitative methods.  Human ethnography is expensive labor intensive and often incomplete. 

Rolling Labs In-Car Digital Ethnography —Enables detailed and cost effective digital in-car ethnography to better understand consumer behavior patterns. Time-stamped video is merged with car and smartphone sensor data logs to paint a detailed holistic picture of consumer behavior both in and out of the car. 

Automation Adds Scale & Flexibility —Leverages passive unobtrusive data capture to generate robust data points. Automation extracts more information with less labor. Machine vision helps to identify human activity log the frequency and duration of relevant actions and generate video clips for further human coding.  Data analysis tools identify patterns and generate visualizations to tell a more complete story.

name: Neurospire
description:
We know that research methodologies based on self-report (focus groups and surveys) are not always the best way to discover consumer insights. In fact neuroscientists estimate that about 90% of decision-making occurs at the subconscious level.

Neuromarketing research which involves the use of brain-imaging technology to measure subconscious emotional responses has been shown in many cases to be more predictive of consumer behavior than traditional methods. Despite the repeated in-market validation of neuromarketing metrics in recent years neuromarketing remains an underutilized research methodology due to its associated costs complexity and logistical issues. NeuroSpire changes this with our technology platform.

NeuroSpire software acquires brainwave data from electroencephalography (EEG) devices which research participants wear while being exposed to various marketing/media communications. Our software then analyzes this raw EEG data and transforms it into second-by-second measures of participants’ subconscious emotional responses. This information gets uploaded to the cloud where researchers use an interactive online dashboard to view and search through study results. Researchers can use this information to see for instance exactly where in a television commercial emotional engagement drops off or to see which new packaging design is going to best elevate consumers’ purchase intent.

name: Scenario DNA
description:
At scenarioDNA we use the term culture mapping in its anthropological sense -- monitoring signs symbols and language put out into the world by everyday people. This differs from persona development.

Instead our methodology involves a semiotic matrix that we use to plot unstructured data in order to see how it sits against self and society and time. It’s an inductive approach that lets us form and explore theories and hypotheses. We’ve been patterning data as such since 2001.

Data to us means the collective output of people archetypes and groups. They are constantly evolving language and unwittingly conspiring. We don’t need to know how people identify themselves to understand their collective phenomenon. Research subjects can maintain their anonymity.

Their self-expression and relation to society qualifies where their data is placed on our matrix. For example not all gluten-free choosers are the same. Have they had their arms twisted? Are they doing it to sound smarter? Is it simply a healthy choice? Or is this a paleo lifestyle? Plotting the data shows us what it all means.

Transferring our hand­-coding to a semiotic “machine” that processes data will free up time to devote to analysis and allow us to grow.

name: Chatty Insights
description: Chatty is the research OS for mid-size challenger brands. We replace the platform-as-destination model with a conversational research brain that lives where the team already works, starting with Microsoft Teams. Three things make us different: an always-on consumer knowledge graph that grows with every study a brand runs, a human-in-the-loop methodology that keeps research expertise in the loop at the moments that matter, and a platformless interface that meets researchers in their existing tools instead of forcing yet another login. We compress what used to be six-week qualitative research projects into days, while accumulating institutional consumer knowledge that previously got lost in scattered decks and shared drives. The result is a research function that runs on autopilot for the predictable work, with humans available for the work that requires judgment.

how_it_works: Chatty runs end-to-end qualitative research using AI-moderated conversational interviews. Brands give us a research brief; we recruit participants, conduct interviews via our conversational AI moderator, analyze responses, and deliver structured insight reports plus exec-ready presentations. Every completed study automatically feeds a per-customer knowledge graph that grows over time. Our target audience is research managers at mid-size challenger brands with active research functions but limited capacity. What disrupts the existing model: research becomes always-on rather than project-based, lives inside Microsoft Teams rather than a separate platform, captures institutional memory rather than producing decks that get archived. Where current AI research vendors sell tools researchers must learn to use, we deliver finished research with software-grade margins and methodological rigor, accessed through tools the team already uses every day.
business_need: Mid-size challenger brands have a research budget but lack the capacity to run consumer research at the speed and frequency their growth requires. Traditional agencies are too slow and too expensive for the volume needed. AI research platforms force the research team to learn new tools, which research teams won't do, leaving expensive software shelf-ware. Knowledge fragmentation is the parallel pain: every study gets archived as a deck, institutional memory leaks with every reorg, and the same research questions get asked across teams independently. There is no current solution that combines the speed of AI-native research, the trust of human-led methodology, the always-on accumulation of institutional consumer knowledge, and delivery inside the tools brand teams already use. We address all four together.

We address all four together.
case_study:
Real case study: Campari Group
Campari Group, a publicly traded global spirits company, partnered with Chatty for AI-moderated qualitative research on consumer preferences across multiple brands and markets. We delivered structured insight reports in days rather than the six to eight weeks typical for traditional research agencies. The work spanned multiple countries and languages, with the AI moderator probing dynamically based on participant responses in real time. Reports cited every insight back to the source interview, giving Campari's research team full traceability for internal stakeholder presentations. The relationship has expanded across multiple brands and markets, with the team now in active discussions to convert from project-based engagements into a recurring annual partnership and to integrate Chatty with their internal knowledge systems.
Hypothetical deployment for a mid-market challenger brand
A research manager at a mid-size consumer electronics company opens Microsoft Teams. They type "I need to understand why our new product launch is resonating with Gen Z but not with millennials." Chatty's conversational interface picks up the brief, asks clarifying questions, and proposes a study design. The research manager approves. Behind the scenes, Chatty recruits participants, runs AI-moderated qualitative interviews across both segments, transcribes and analyzes responses, and generates a structured report plus a presentation deck. The research manager reviews the output inside Teams without ever opening a separate platform. The findings flow into the company's growing consumer knowledge graph, where they connect to prior studies. Six months later, when a new product is in development, the team queries the graph directly through Teams and gets answers grounded in their accumulated consumer understanding without commissioning a new study.

name: Glaut
description: Glaut is a research platform that provides real-time fraud prevention and engagement scoring directly during the interview process. While most data-quality solutions focus on post-field cleaning or panel-level controls, Glaut detects low-quality or fraudulent behavior in real-time. Our integrated agents identify speeding, incoherent open-ended responses, copy-paste patterns, and uncooperative behavior as they occur, enabling researchers to remove bad actors before incentives are paid and before unusable data is added to the dataset. The result is higher-quality insights, faster analysis, and more reliable consumer understanding, especially in online environments where fraud has become widespread.
how_it_works:
Glaut functions as an AI-moderated research and data-quality layer that runs during the interview, not afterward. Our fraud prevention agents operate in real time across both open-ended and structured question formats.
-Gibberish and uncooperative response detection: when respondents give nonsensical text, irrelevant answers, or show clear disengagement, the agent can end the interview and flag the respondent ID. 
- Speeding and pattern-based behavior checks: on closed-ended grids and matrix questions, Glaut tracks response times, rushing behavior, and repetitive answering patterns to identify professional survey fraudsters.
- Engagement-quality scoring: each interview is tagged with an engagement level (high, medium, low), enabling teams to filter datasets transparently instead of using subjective cleaning rules. 
- Copy-paste prevention by default. 
- Voice-only mode as a structural defense: voice-based interviewing naturally prevents many bot-driven fraud behaviors.
business_need:
The global insights industry is facing a structural breakdown in trust in online survey data, especially amid the rise of synthetic data.
Independent audits indicate that roughly half of online panel responses may be fraudulent or unusable. In open-link surveys, usable response rates have fallen in some instances from 75% to as low as 10%, mainly due to professional fraud and automation.
At the same time, industry standards now treat identity validation, de-duplication, and engagement measurement as core requirements for online sample quality.
Yet most current workflows still depend on post-hoc cleaning, meaning brands often pay for bad data, find quality issues too late, and base decisions on distorted signals.
Glaut addresses this unmet need by integrating fraud detection directly into the interview process, preventing low-quality data from entering the research entirely.
business_potential: Glaut meets the criteria of originality, scalability, market potential, and ease of implementation. Our unique approach is real-time, in-interview fraud prevention rather than post-field cleaning. The market opportunity is substantial: the survey fraud detection and data integrity segment is already valued at $4.34 billion in 2025 and is projected to reach $15.77 billion by 2033 (17.5% CAGR). Glaut functions as a cloud-based scoring layer that can integrate via iframes, redirect-based gateways, or panel/router deployments. By reducing unusable completes before incentives are paid, the ROI is immediate, making adoption practical for panels, enterprises, and research teams.
case_study:
A market research agency is commissioned to run a multi-country consumer study using online access panels. Historically, the agency would collect full quotas, then spend days cleaning the data, removing speeders, straight-liners, and nonsensical open-ends. Incentives would already be paid, timelines delayed, and clients left questioning data reliability. With Glaut, the agency activates data-quality and fraud-prevention agents directly within the interview during project setup. As respondents progress, the system evaluates engagement and behavioral signals continuously. If a participant provides gibberish in open-ended questions, irrelevant answers, or shows clear disengagement, the uncooperative agent terminates the interview in real time and flags the respondent ID. Speeding on matrix questions is detected through time-to-answer and pattern analysis. Automation-like behavior and repetitive response structures are identified during completion. Copy-paste inputs are blocked by default, and interviews are labeled high, medium, or low engagement for transparent filtering.

Instead of cleaning after fieldwork, the agency prevents low-integrity data from entering the dataset in the first place. Only engaged, authentic responses move forward to analysis.

For panel and sample providers, Glaut can be deployed at the router or entry point. Respondents are screened through the quality agents before entering client surveys. Fraudsters and professional survey takers can be removed at the source, reducing chargebacks and protecting panel reputation across multiple projects.

These are not theoretical scenarios. Clients are already activating these agents in live research. When the uncooperative detector is enabled, an average of 40% of the initial panel is removed due to engagement-quality concerns, with an additional 20% expelled for clear fraudster behavior. This intervention happens before incentives are paid and before the dataset is finalized.

name: AI-Native Research Agency
description:
Consumer research used to take 8 weeks and cost $25,000–$50,000 per project. We deliver the same quality in 2 days, at a fraction of the cost.
Chatty Insights is an AI-native research agency. We handle everything brands currently outsource to traditional agencies: participant recruiting, AI-moderated in-depth interviews, qualitative analysis, and polished insight reports, all automated behind the scenes, invisible to the client.
What makes us different is the combination of business model and technology. Our AI moderator conducts dynamic conversational interviews, probing and adapting in real time based on what each participant says, the same way a skilled human moderator would. Then our pipeline automatically transcribes, codes, themes, and synthesizes findings into structured reports ready for brand decision-making.
The result: research managers at publicly traded companies like Campari and Flowers Foods get the finished report on their desk in days, not weeks, without changing how
how_it_works:
A client briefs us on their research question, a product launch, a brand perception study, a market entry. From there, our platform takes over entirely.
We recruit participants across target demographics and markets (we've run studies in 9+ countries). Our AI moderator conducts one-on-one conversational interviews, dynamically probing based on each response rather than following a rigid script. This produces the depth and nuance of qualitative research at the speed and scale of quantitative methods.
The interview data feeds directly into our analysis pipeline: transcription, thematic coding, sentiment analysis, and insight synthesis, all automated. Our system then generates structured insight reports that mirror what a senior researcher would produce, formatted for brand strategy teams.
Our target audience is research managers and insights directors at enterprise consumer brands, specifically CPG, food & beverage, and retail companies that currently rely on traditional agencies.
business_need:
Qualitative consumer research is one of the most valuable inputs brand teams have, and one of the most broken processes in market research. The status quo requires 6–8 weeks, $25K–$50K per study, and coordination across recruiting firms, moderators, analysts, and report writers. The result is that brands make major decisions, product launches, repositioning, campaign pivots, without the consumer insight they need, because by the time the research arrives, the window has closed.
The insights industry has tried to solve this with DIY platforms and survey tools, but enterprise research managers don't use them. They're too busy, too risk-averse about quality, and too accountable for the outputs they present to leadership. What the market actually needs is an agency that delivers the finished product, but one built on AI infrastructure rather than human labor. That's the gap nobody has filled. We have.
business_potential:
Originality: There is no AI-native research agency operating at the enterprise level with our model. Traditional agencies use humans end-to-end. AI platforms offload work to clients. We do neither, we automate the full research pipeline and deliver the outcome. This is a genuinely new category.
Market Potential: The global market research industry is worth approximately $80B, with qualitative research representing roughly a third. It's fragmented across thousands of small agencies doing manual work, exactly the type of market that AI can consolidate. We don't need a large share to build a significant business.
Scalability: Our cost structure is the core of our model. A 4-person team today serves publicly traded multinationals across 9 countries. Adding more clients doesn't require adding proportional headcount, it requires better AI infrastructure. We can 10x revenue without 10x the people.
Ease of Implementation: From the buyer's perspective, there is zero friction.
case_study:
A global premium spirits company with 50+ brands across 190 countries needed to decode a complex sensory topic across six cultures, in under a week. Their Global Insights Manager had a high-stakes internal workshop on the calendar and no time for a traditional agency process.

They called us. We handled everything.

Within four days, Chatty Insights recruited participants, ran AI-moderated in-depth interviews across six countries, Italy, Brazil, Germany, the UK, the US, and Argentina, and delivered a fully synthesized report. 180 interviews. Six languages. Four days. No local moderators, no translation delays, no manual analysis. The client briefed us on the research question and received the findings. They never logged into a platform.

Our AI moderator conducted each interview dynamically, probing based on what participants actually said. This is what produced the depth: US consumers described the flavor as something to push through; Italians framed the same sensation as a marker of sophistication. Cultural distinctions that traditional cross-market studies take weeks to surface, we surfaced them in days.

"We weren't just getting survey data, we were getting the 'why,'" the Global Insights Manager said. "Walking into that workshop with fresh data from six markets gave us a completely different level of authority."

A six-country qualitative study at a traditional agency would have taken 6–8 weeks and cost $40K–$60K. We delivered it in four days at a fraction of the cost, with 70–80% gross margins on our end.

For a team that previously saw research as a bottleneck, this changed the relationship entirely. The insights arrived fast enough to actually shape the strategy, not validate it after the fact. That's what Chatty Insights is built to do.

name: AddMaple
description:
AddMaple helps teams scale insight impact into people intelligence (customers, prospects, etc.) that informs marketing, sales, growth, product, and leadership. It pulls feedback from surveys, trackers, online reviews, Reddit forums, support logs, and data lakes, then makes it visually explorable as interactive charts and tables, so teams can answer business questions the same day through branded decks or interactive story dashboards that are fully editable and on-brand.

Our AI is powered by a proprietary statistical engine. Skip DP delays and one-off tests as AddMaple surfaces the most meaningful relationships in your data, runs key driver analysis and clustering, and speeds up the path from finding to evidence to meaning. It also codes text into themes and sentiment to spot bug mentions and highlight what customers like or dislike, linked back to scores to get to the 'why'.

The result is triangulated, statistically tested understanding teams can trust, communicate, and act on.
how_it_works:
AddMaple pulls in mixed feedback from surveys, trackers, support logs, reviews, social/community channels, competitor chatter, and internal data to help insight teams answer business questions with data they have - fast.

It turns data into an interactive, explore-ready, statistically testable workspace. Teams don’t wait for data processing tables. From the second data lands, they can build banners, slice and cut the dataset, and answer questions as they arise. Key drivers and clustering work across mixed data types, including categorical and multi-category. Expand any variable to see what’s most related, ranked by statistical strength.

AddMaple’s AI codes open-ended feedback into themes and sentiment, linked to verbatim excerpts. Those themes become variables in the stats engine, showing which segments mention what most and which themes link to high or low scores. The AI agent answers using the stats and chart engine, producing deterministic, editable outputs, not prompt ping-pong.
business_need:
Businesses need to understand their customer segments, what each group needs, what frustrates them, and what drives behavior. But customer feedback isn’t in one place. It’s fragmented across surveys, trackers, reviews, support logs, social channels, and internal systems.

The missing piece is exploration. Today, insight workflows rely on stop-start data processing cycles - specify cuts, wait for tables, specify more cuts. Scrolling, not exploring.

AI is often hailed as the fix, but black-box AI gives answers we can’t fully trust, and charts we can’t edit. Meanwhile, most text analysis stays high level and disconnected from quant, so the links between themes and scores go undiscovered. 

The unmet need is an explainable, research-grade exploration layer that unifies mixed feedback, connects qual to quant with statistically grounded tests, and produces editable outputs as decks and rich story dashboards (images, video, narrative) so stakeholders understand the “aha” and can act on it.
business_potential:
Originality of concept
AddMaple combines a proprietary statistical engine with traceable AI workflows and editable outputs, so researchers can explore faster, follow statistically grounded relationships, ask an AI Agent questions, and stay in control.

Market potential
AddMaple serves customer insights, CX, product, and data teams, plus market research consultants and agencies. They need to spend less time cleaning and processing data and more time extracting meaning. AddMaple also unifies fragmented feedback into an insight hub across surveys, trackers, social, and support tickets, making demand broad and recurring.

Scalability and ease of implementation
AddMaple scales from solo analysts to teams by offering one tool and one learning curve across data types. Setup is lightweight: connect sources and start exploring immediately, with no scripts or table requests. An in-product AI Guide supports adoption, while an AI Agent runs analysis alongside the researcher.
case_study:
Luiza Fortuna at Superstruct
Superstruct is the second largest entertainment corporation globally. Luiza uses AddMaple to analyze festival attendee feedback across 60+ multi-national events, with surveys run by local organizers using different tools including Typeform, Decipher, and Alchemer, and submitted in multiple languages. AddMaple unified the fragmented data into one workspace, coded open-ended responses into comparable themes across languages, surfaced key improvement areas, and connected qual themes back to quant metrics (including artist mentions for unaided awareness testing).

Susan Baier at Audience Audit
Susan switched from Tableau after ~20 years because AddMaple removed at least 1 month of data cleaning and back-and-forth with data processing. She connects her Alchemer data and explores it the same day and builds explorable dashboards in AddMaple for her clients to use to promote their thought leadership. Susan also uses our “Guess Charts” as mini engagement nuggets on LinkedIn that links viewers to explorable dashboards, to help her clients grow their following and their businesses.

Greenpeace International
Greenpeace International use AddMaple to understand public opinion across a range of dataset, topics and regions. They moved from a Tableau setup that was slow to load and costly to build. AddMaple enables teams across the organization to analyze mixed data types quickly and share findings through dashboards that make insight accessible beyond the analyst. They also use AddMaple internal information sharing. 

Specialized
Specialized uses AddMaple to explore their ongoing distributor feedback survey across distribution centres in multiple languages. AddMaple helps them bring text and quant feedback into one coherent view to compare centres consistently, and extract clear, actionable themes and patterns across the whole dataset.

name: Agentic Planogram Discovery Platform
description:
Over the past 3 years, we have been accumulating planogram data (which products sit on which shelves, in which stores, and with how much space) from grocery retailers, cleaning and organising this data into a consistent data model, and providing access to that for CPGs to visualise their products on shelf as if they were a shopper and analyze where their products need to be.

This organisation of the data has now meant that we can build an agentic discovery platform (an MCP - similar to a ChatGPT or Claude) on top of it.

This changes the way that CPGs and retailers can interact with their planogram data, moving away from messy spreadsheets and countless dashboards and into conversational opportunity discovery, and providing 10x efficiencies into what you can discover from these datasets (e.g. new store opportunities, better layout configurations, supply chain efficiencies).

This is the future of shopper insights and category management in store interactivity and planning.
how_it_works:
Our Planogram Publisher Agentic Analytics MCP server enables sophisticated queries to be built, executed and visualized directly based on a planogram dataset that now totals >9.1billion rows of data across >6,000 retail stores across the globe.

For a user, the experience is simple:
1) Describe what you want in natural language
2) LLM builds a query using a detailed description of the data available
3) Claude executes the query & retrieves the result (max 1000 rows)
4) Proceed to draw a graph / ask more questions

Internally, we have used this for our own analysis and a 10x gain in the speed of delivery of projects (identifying store layout improvements, space changes, heatmapping, stock availability options, and more). 

We have recently released this across our CPG client base for further testing - described as "a game-changer for planogram data interactivity", and "infinite data analysts on tap".
business_need:
Global insights teams previously had a problem - "what does my product look like in-store?" When asking this question from remote headquarters, it was virtually impossible to see, unless local market teams could go out and take photos of shelves of a few stores (a slow, costly, and inaccurate process). 

We've solved this.

Wherever you are in the world, you can ask your planogram discovery agent to show you your most common plan, or for a specific local store, and you can see a shelf visualisation of your store, the analytics behind it (e.g. share of shelf, sales heatmapping on-shelf, availability metrics), and how that has changed over time.
business_potential:
We are currently in beta testing throughout February and March with Red Bull, Diageo, PepsiCo and Nestle. We're starting in the UK with planogram data coverage >60% of the stores in the market, before this launches in the US in March as well.

This is a fundamental change - what was a dataset that was previously only available to supply chain teams is now readily available for insights, category management, shopper marketing and field sales teams to do their jobs better, with better visibility, and providing CPGs and retailers with a better way of focusing on their shoppers.
case_study: Red Bull used our planogram discovery agent to identify specific under-performing stores, driven by out-of-stock issues where Red Bull products did not have enough space on-shelf. The retailer was struggling to fill the shelves in these 250 stores where Energy Drinks were most popular, and an increase in space dedicated to Red Bull drove a 7% increase in sales in the total Soft Drinks category.

name: ArchetypeID
description:
ArchetypeID develops Virtual Twins that simulate the psychological drivers of consumer behavior. While standard industry digital twins aggregate static demographic data to model what consumers do, our architecture models the underlying why. This is driven by our proprietary engine, a framework integrating over 75 behavioral science elements to construct personas with hyper-localized cultural fidelity. 

To maintain relevance, we utilize a GraphRAG methodology. This provides the Virtual Twins with persistent memory, allowing them to ingest updated consumer data, connect complex relational nodes, and evolve. We also apply systemic constraints to remove the inherent positive bias typical of baseline LLMs, ensuring objective, critical feedback. 

Because of this behavioral depth, the platform functions as a Synthetic Focus Group. Researchers use it to test concepts, isolate emotional motivators, and calculate predictive performance scores for creative variants prior to deployment.
how_it_works:
ArchetypeID is a B2B SaaS platform. we construct Virtual Twins using a GraphRAG framework to map 75+ behavioral elements against continuous consumer data. Users deploy these twins in Synthetic Focus Groups to test concepts, generating qualitative verbatims, friction points, and predictive scores.
While 1st Gen synthetic panels use basic prompting to mimic demographics, ArchetypeID embeds structural psychological depth. Integrating behavioral science, localized nuances, and neutralizing LLM positive bias, we isolate subconscious attitudes—bypassing the shallowness of early synthetics and the social bias of human panels.
The target audience spans enterprise insights, strategy, and marketing teams, plus their agencies.
We shift qualitative research from manual consulting to an on-demand utility. Conventional panels require weeks of logistics; we execute simulations in minutes. Enabling teams to iteratively validate pre-launch and embed behavioral insights directly into daily workflows.
business_need:
The global insights sector lacks a method to capture deep qualitative data without the delays of human panels or the flaws of early AI. Traditional focus groups suffer from social desirability and researcher biases, causing participants to alter responses based on group dynamics. Conversely, standard LLMs exhibit inherent positive bias—generating agreeable, rather than critical, feedback.

ArchetypeID resolves this dual gap. Our Synthetic Focus Groups eliminate human groupthink by isolating the testing environment. Concurrently, we apply systemic constraints to remove the positive bias of baseline LLMs, forcing Virtual Twins to articulate authentic friction and objections. By mitigating both sociological and architectural biases, the platform delivers objective, high-fidelity insights at operational speed.
business_potential:
ArchetypeID demonstrates viability across four core criteria:
Originality: Moving beyond early AI that merely mimics language, our Prometheus Engine uses GraphRAG to map 75+ behavioral elements and neutralize LLM positive bias, enabling true psychological analysis.
Market Potential: Targeting the $217B marketing intelligence sector, we address the 73% of enterprise ad spend wasted on poor creative. Our $890K ARR confirms strong B2B demand for predictive insights.
Scalability: As a SaaS platform, it executes concurrent Synthetic Focus Groups across complex, localized segments in minutes, decoupling research from the physical constraints of manual consulting.
Ease of Implementation: The platform is a secure, turnkey utility integrating into existing workflows without requiring data science expertise.
This validity is recognized by our selection as a Technology Association of Georgia (TAG) Top 10 Innovative Company.
case_study:
ArchetypeID is a turnkey SaaS utility. Users define audience parameters, prompting the Prometheus Engine to construct Virtual Twins embedded with 75+ behavioral elements via GraphRAG. Deployed into Synthetic Focus Groups, these twins react to stimuli like ad copy or packaging, outputting qualitative verbatims, psychological friction points, and predictive scores.

Case Study: Global CPG Soup Brand A global CPG enterprise faced stagnant growth for a heritage soup in Mexico. Traditional panels required months, while early AI yielded generic translations lacking cultural depth.

The brand executed 12 concurrent Synthetic Focus Groups via ArchetypeID in hours. The platform generated Virtual Twins utilizing hyper-localized cultural and regional nuancests. With LLM positive bias neutralized, the twins delivered critical, unvarnished feedback, firmly rejecting the pristine, idealized domestic settings in current ads as inauthentic.

The simulation isolated a core psychological conflict between the cultural ideal of "homemade" care and modern time constraints. Consumers felt latent guilt using convenience products. Data revealed they viewed the soup not as a finished meal, but as a "base segura" (safe base) for adding a personal touch.

This shifted brand positioning from "convenience" to a "partner in care" enabling co-creation. Mitigating this guilt unlocked a projected $6M–$15.5M annual revenue stream. Furthermore, the rapid insight generation catalyzed five distinct product innovations, validating commercial viability in a single afternoon.

name: Assessment Labs
description:
Our innovation transforms short voice messages — recorded responses to an open-ended question — into measurable behavioral risk indicators using AI and statistical modeling. By analyzing spontaneous speech rather than self-reported answers typical of psychometric questionnaires, we identify behavioral patterns associated with fraud propensity and credit default risk within minutes.

Unlike traditional credit models that rely heavily on historical financial data, our approach adds a behavioral layer that works even for thin-file or underbanked populations. The system continuously learns from outcome feedback, strengthening correlations between speech patterns and financial performance.

This enables faster risk classification, automated scoring, and scalable portfolio insights — improving credit decisions without increasing customer friction.
how_it_works:
Our solution invites individuals to answer a single open-ended question using a short voice message (~2 min) on their mobile device. The audio is then transcribed and analyzed by the AI for behavioral features. Patterns linked to risk, trustworthiness, and decision behavior are identified, producing automated scores within minutes. These scores are then statistically classified and outliers are flagged.

For financial institutions and digital platforms, this enables a deeper understanding of customer intent and behavioral risk beyond traditional data. It improves engagement by using a natural, low-friction interaction that feels more human than form-based questionnaires.

The primary users are fintechs, lenders, insurers, and digital platforms serving thin-file or underbanked populations. Delivered via API or embedded workflow, the solution is faster than manual assessments and reveals behavioral insights unattainable through conventional credit and survey methods.
business_need:
Our solution addresses a critical gap in the global insights ecosystem: the inability to reliably understand behavioral intent and risk when traditional data is limited, biased, or self-reported. Surveys, panels, and psychometric questionnaires depend on conscious responses and are vulnerable to social desirability bias, while transactional and demographic data fail to capture motivation, trust, and decision behavior.

By analyzing spontaneous voice responses, our approach reveals behavioral signals that individuals do not consciously filter, enabling more authentic insight into attitudes, credibility, and risk propensity. This creates a new layer of understanding that is especially valuable in emerging markets and among thin-file populations, where conventional data is scarce.

The result is faster, more inclusive, and behaviorally grounded insight generation — enabling organizations to make better decisions, reduce risk, and engage people more effectively.
business_potential:
Our solution introduces an original behavioral data layer derived from spontaneous voice responses, transforming natural language into measurable indicators of trust, intent, and risk. This approach expands insight generation beyond self-reported surveys and traditional financial or demographic data.

Market potential is significant: financial services, insurers, digital platforms, and research organizations all face growing pressure to understand behavior, reduce risk, and include thin-file populations. Because the interaction is simple and mobile-first, it can be deployed globally across diverse markets.

The platform is highly scalable, delivered via API and automated scoring, enabling high-volume processing with minimal marginal cost. Implementation is fast and frictionless, requiring only a short voice response from users and seamless integration into existing digital workflows.
case_study:
Example in Action: “Na Real” App for Microentrepreneurs

Yes, we do have an early adopter pilot that demonstrates Assessment Labs' solution in practice: the Na Real app, created by Crédito Social to help microentrepreneurs manage their businesses day to day while improving their chances of having greater and fairer access to credit.

The app provides tools for tracking sales, expenses, inventory, and cash flow, alongside embedded financial education that strengthens decision-making and business resilience.

At the top of the funnel, aspiring entrepreneurs arrive at a simple landing page where they record a brief voice message answering an open-ended behavioral question and another one describing their business idea and main challenge. Within minutes, they receive a free, personalized mini business plan with clear, practical next steps. They are then invited to download the app to begin managing their business.

Through API integration, Assessment Labs receives the voice response and applies AI and statistical modeling to extract behavioral indicators correlated with creditworthiness — including planning, discipline, consistency, risk awareness, and decision style.

As entrepreneurs continue using the app, structured operational data accumulates over time. When combined with behavioral analysis, this creates a multidimensional profile of reliability, risk, and business performance.

With user consent, fintechs, MFIs, and banks can integrate these insights directly into onboarding and credit workflows. The result is reduced uncertainty, improved risk assessment, and more inclusive lending — particularly for thin-file borrowers.

The Na Real pilot illustrates how voice-based behavioral intelligence, combined with real operating data, can expand access to credit at better rates while strengthening financial education, business sustainability, and economic inclusion in emerging markets.

name: BluePill AI

how_it_works:

Using our proprietary AI and behavioral simulation technology, we model how consumers actually make decisions - capturing trade-offs, heuristics, and context rather than just demographics or stated opinions.
The models are continuously validated against live human studies, ensuring the outputs reflect real consumer behavior, not synthetic guesswork.

business_potential:
Originality

Market potential
The global consumer insights and market research market exceeds $80B annually, with CPG brands alone running hundreds of tests per year across innovation, packaging, and messaging - creating strong, recurring demand for faster and cheaper insight generation.
