name: CX Behaviour Ladder
description: The CX Behaviour Ladder is a proprietary customer experience measurement framework that redefines how advocacy and loyalty are understood by moving beyond intent‑based metrics such as NPS. Rather than assuming that positive sentiment equates to real advocacy or future value, the framework introduces a laddered progression that validates whether customer intent translates into actual behaviour and whether that behaviour is sustainable over time. By explicitly separating what customers say they will do, what they have actually done, and what they plan to do next, the CX Behaviour Ladder™ transforms CX measurement from a single attitudinal score into a behaviour‑validated system that reveals hidden risk, unrealised advocacy potential, and the true strength of customer relationships. It represents a shift from score‑tracking to evidence‑based CX diagnosis, enabling organisations to measure advocacy as a proven action rather than an assumed outcome.
how_it_works: The CX Behaviour Ladder is a lightweight framework that integrates into existing NPS or VoC surveys to validate whether customer sentiment translates into real behaviour and sustained usage. It works by layering two sequenced questions to capture three signals: intent, action, and retention. This structure enables brands to distinguish assumed advocacy from proven advocacy and to identify whether positive sentiment is commercially sustainable. Customers are classified into clearly defined segments, improving how organisations understand customer experiences, prioritise engagement strategies, and identify hidden risk or unrealised value. Designed for insights teams, CX leaders, and senior decision‑makers in medium to large organisations, the framework is delivered as a fast‑to‑deploy methodology rather than a standalone platform. Its disruptive value lies in enabling insights that conventional NPS approaches cannot - separating sentiment from behaviour and loyalty from retention risk.
business_need: A key unmet need in the global CX and insights space is a practical way to operationalise the well‑recognised gap between customer intent and actual behaviour. While the industry widely relies on intent‑based measures such as NPS to represent loyalty, it also acknowledges that attitudinal loyalty (how customers feel) differs from behavioural loyalty (what customers do). Yet most VoC programmes still treat promoters as a proxy for advocacy, without validating real recommendation behaviour or stress‑testing loyalty through a retention lens. As a result, organisations can optimise CX scores while missing hidden churn risk and unrealised advocacy potential. The CX Behaviour Ladder addresses this gap by adding lightweight behavioural validation and retention lenses, transforming NPS from a headline metric into decision‑ready insight on true advocacy, risk, and engagement priorities.
business_potential: The CX Behaviour Ladder demonstrates strong business potential through its originality, scalability, and ease of adoption within a mature global CX ecosystem. Its originality lies in structurally resolving a widely acknowledged but unresolved problem in CX measurement: the gap between customer intent and actual behaviour, without requiring new technology or complex data integration. Market potential is significant, as the framework enhances the value of one of the most widely used CX metrics globally (NPS), making it immediately relevant across industries and geographies. The model is highly scalable, as it can be applied consistently across trackers, brands, and markets using standard survey data. Ease of implementation is a key differentiator: the CX Behaviour Ladder integrates into existing VoC programmes with minimal cost, disruption, or training, enabling rapid adoption and long‑term relevance as organisations seek more accountable, decision‑ready CX measurement.
case_study: A practical application of the CX Behaviour Ladder can be seen within a bNPS tracking programme we conduct for one of the largest telecommunications brands in South Africa (for consumers and businesses) where the framework has been embedded into their globally mandated Brand Net Promoter Score (bNPS) tracker to enhance interpretation and actionability. In this context, the framework is deployed by layering two additional questions: validated advocacy (whether a customer has actually recommended the brand) and retention intent (are they intending on using the brand again in the future) onto the existing bNPS survey structure. This allows the business to move beyond headline NPS trends and classify customers into behaviour‑based segments such as true advocates, latent promoters, and advocates at risk. Insights are then analysed by region, segment, and line of business, enabling our client to be able to identify where advocacy is not converting into action, where advocacy exists but retention is fragile, and where engagement should be prioritised. While still early in its application, this use case demonstrates how the CX Behaviour Ladder can be operationalised within a large‑scale, globally standardised tracker without disrupting methodology, tooling, or reporting cadence, illustrating its practicality and scalability for enterprise CX programmes.

name: Cheetah Research AI
description: Cheetah Research AI is an end-to-end conversational research platform built to embody the mindset and professional standards of an experienced researcher operating at their highest level. Developed from the real need of a research agency to scale analytical capacity without compromising rigor, it systematizes disciplined judgment across study design, data collection, quality control, analysis and reporting.Its structured study frameworks produce actionable outputs rather than descriptive summaries, combining conversational qualitative depth with quantitative measurement to explain not only what is happening, but why.Through its analytical assistant, Socrates, users can extend analysis beyond the initial report testing assumptions and exploring interpretations, always within data limits. The platform draws on a proprietary knowledge base informed by more than 5,000 real studies. In essence, Cheetah Research is not an AI tool, it is experienced research judgment amplified by technology.
how_it_works: The process begins with selecting a study type from a structured professional menu (CX, NPS, Brand Health, Psychographics and more). Researchers define objectives and questions while the platform refines and optimizes them before launch. Studies can be tested and deployed via direct links, QR codes or consumer panels. Data is collected through configurable virtual interviewer logic shaped by curated professional experience, probing for consistency and applying embedded quality controls. The platform can also analyze external datasets, social media conversations and focus group transcripts within the same frameworks. Outputs move beyond metrics toward structured interpretation. Through Socrates, teams test assumptions and enables brands to identify behavioral drivers, reduce internal speculation and make faster, evidence-based strategic decisions transforming research from a static report into an active decision system.Designed for in-house insight teams, agencies and executive leaders.
business_need: Cheetah Research delivers deeper, faster and actionable insights without sacrificing methodological rigor. Companies are under constant pressure to move faster with fewer resources, often forcing a trade-off between speed and analytical depth. Cheetah Research was built to remove that compromise allowing accelerated insight generation without weakening methodological standards. The platform unifies quantitative measurement and structured conversational analysis in a single professional environment. It transforms both newly collected and externally sourced data, including prior quantitative studies, qualitative projects and focus group transcripts into coherent, interrogable insight. By codifying agency-level expertise and transmitting research best practices directly to users, Cheetah Research expands analytical capacity while preserving professional discipline turning research into a multidimensional, executive-ready decision system.
business_potential:
Cheetah Research is original in that it systematizes experienced research judgment within a scalable conversational platform, embedding methodological guardrails and structured analytical frameworks directly into the workflow.
Its market potential spans in-house insights teams, research agencies and independent professionals seeking to expand analytical capacity without increasing headcount. As organizations integrate AI into research functions, demand for disciplined, professional analytical environments continues to grow globally.

Designed as a multi-tenant platform, it enables rapid onboarding of dedicated client accounts, each customizable with branded interfaces and white-label configurations. A transparent token-based model links revenue directly to research scope, enabling predictable scaling.

As a configurable platform rather than a bespoke service, it integrates easily into existing workflows and supports reanalysis of externally generated studies.
case_study:
Case#1 Retail Format Repositioning: Supermercado La Colonia (Honduras) faced underperformance in its new formats “Mi Súper” and “Súper Cerca,” each targeting different HHI segments. The goal was to understand what was driving results beyond surface metrics.Cheetah Research deployed conversational surveys with shoppers and nearby non-shoppers, linking open feedback to demographic and behavioral data. Analysis showed that “Mi Súper” worked as a proximity-driven convenience option for lower-income segments, while “Súper Cerca” failed to resonate with higher-income consumers, who perceived it as an unclear hybrid with limited assortment and parking constraints.Using Socrates in executive sessions, leaders explored friction points in real time, shifting strategy from marketing tweaks to positioning, assortment and infrastructure adjustments.
Case#2 From QR Scores to Root-Cause Insight: Banco Ficohsa relied on a QR-based five-question survey to measure post-service satisfaction. While efficient, it produced scores without explaining the drivers behind them. The bank replaced it with a conversational interview that preserved the same ratings for  continuity. Customers first described their experience in their own words before responding to structured questions. This shift revealed that low scores were often driven by perceived staff attitude rather than process delays. The conversational method exposed behavioral causes and service inconsistencies, transforming a passive feedback metric into an actionable diagnostic tool.

name: Compeers AI Corporation
description:
Insight teams run on a patchwork of tools. Each tool handles a task, doesn’t  talk to each other, and the researcher spends as much time managing the stack as doing meaningful work. 
Compeers AI is an end-to-end insights platform where AI works alongside researchers through every step of any qual or quant project - from planning and fieldwork to analysis and reporting. It brings in past projects and external data through Savant, so analysis is built on the full picture, not a slice of it.
Coherence makes us unique. Researcher adds context, AI handles execution and unbiased, 1st-draft reports. It holds context within and across the projects, so steps are aware of what came before, resulting in rigorous analysis grounded in context.
Researchers spend less time on processes; more time on strategic work. Outputs are faster, consistent across projects and teams, and fully traceable, so there's no guessing at how a finding was reached.
Compeers is built for how insights should get done.
how_it_works:
Compeers AI works by keeping researchers in control while AI executes and does the data heavy lifting. It ingests briefs, past research, and external data, structures the workflow, handles execution across every stage, and delivers unbiased, first-draft reports. At each step researchers review, revise and approve. 

The target audience is corporate insights teams - typically lean teams with high research volume and limited bandwidth. These are teams that need to do more without adding headcount.

What makes the go-to-market different is that Compeers is a platform, not a service or a point solution. It replaces the fragmented stack with a single coherent system. Outputs are grounded in project data and verifiable AI, so there's no black box problem. And because Savant activates institutional knowledge, teams can draw on everything they know, not just the current project data. 

That's a category of insight that conventional methods simply can't deliver at speed and scale.
business_need:
The pressure on the insights teams keeps growing. Stakeholder demand grows; budgets don’t. This leaves teams stretched, relying on point solutions that each solve a single problem. Teams spend more time managing processes than doing meaningful work.
The current tools cohort is mostly point solutions. A tool for transcription. A tool for coding. A tool for charting. Each useful in isolation, none of them connected. Teams still carry the burden of stitching everything together.
What's missing is a coherent system that understands the full research process, works across qual and quant, and keeps the researcher in control without requiring them to manage the technology. A system where AI handles execution and the researcher focuses on strategy.
Compeers AI addresses the system; not features. Insights teams need a platform that handles the full lifecycle across both qual and quant, activates institutional knowledge, and delivers outputs at speed. This is the unmet need Compeers AI fills.
business_potential:
Originality: Compeers is the only platform that manages the full insights lifecycle across qual and quant, with a built-in knowledge activation layer. The combination of end-to-end workflows, Savant and verifiable AI is not available elsewhere.
Market potential: Corporate insights is a $50 billion global market under pressure to produce more with less. The fragmented tool stack is a universal problem. Compeers AI addresses it at the system level, not the feature level.
Scalability: Compeers scales with the team. It absorbs the increased work without adding headcount. Savant compounds in value over time as institutional knowledge accumulates.
Ease of implementation: Compeers is designed to fit into existing research workflows. Onboarding is structured around the team's actual projects. Researchers work in familiar stages; the AI operates in the background. There is no steep learning curve. One of our early customers wrote to me - unprompted - “Compeers is a joy to use.”
case_study:
A Fortune 100 online marketplace needed to understand why consumers were discovering their platform but converting elsewhere. They needed to quantify and assess the role of AI in their purchase journey.

The team ran an N=2,500 quantitative study from kick-off to final reports in five days. Once the questionnaire was finalized by the team (took 4 days), Compeers AI executed the rest of the project and delivered the first-draft reports in one business day. The platform handled every step: 

1. Objectives definition
2. Desk research
3. Questionnaire design
4. Survey instrument programming and accuracy checks
5. Data collection (high incidence rate category, IR=65%)
6. Data cleaning
7. Cross-tabulations
8. Open-ended coding
9. Data deep-dive with driver analysis
10. Data deep-dive with high-impact segment discovery
11. Unbiased first-draft executive summary and reports. 

One coherent workflow - end-to-end.

Deliverables included prioritized decision criteria, unmet needs, AI adoption and trust barriers, and messaging implications.

This is how Compeers is deployed: researchers direct the work and AI executes it. No stitching together tools. No handoffs between team members for specialized work.

One user + Compeers AI…end-to-end. The work got done.

Compeers AI is built to deliver this, consistently, across projects and teams.

After the study was complete, the team used Savant to explore project data alongside a recently published McKinsey report on AI in consumer decision-making and asked: 

"Tell me the difference between "AI-Assisted Buyers" and "Unassisted Decision Makers" in ways other than their use of AI during the purchase journey."

Savant worked for nine minutes and returned a comprehensive answer: a short synthesis, verifiable conclusions, data caveats, and 11 beautiful plots showing segments differences across discovery behavior, decision confidence, pain points, and platform trust.

No additional analysis. No new tools. One question, one system.

name: Converse-in-Q
description:
Converse-in-Q is an AI-driven solution that merges the scale of quantitative surveys with the depth of qualitative interviews. Our innovation replaces static forms with a multilingual conversational bot that conducts dynamic, high-engagement surveys globally.

What makes us unique? Unlike standard AI tools, we use a proprietary hybrid architecture to solve the industry’s biggest hurdle: data privacy. We isolate sensitive personal data via classical survey modules (keeping it away from LLMs) while using our bot for rich, conversational discovery. We then recombine these datasets for secure, holistic analysis.

Core Capabilities:

Speed & Scale: Digital self-administration and AI translation allow for instant, global deployment.

Dual-Layer Analytics: We provide a structured KPI dashboard alongside a multilingual "Query Bot," allowing stakeholders to "chat with their data" to uncover instant insights.
how_it_works:
Converse-in-Q is a tech-enabled insights solution using a proprietary hybrid architecture to deliver qualitative depth at quantitative scale. We deploy customized conversational AI bots that use natural language to probe respondent answers in real-time. To ensure enterprise-grade privacy, we use a secure, non-LLM "vault" for sensitive PII, isolating it from the AI engine. We then recombine these streams for a holistic, secure analysis.
Brand Impact: Brands move from rigid, one-way surveys to active dialogue. This uncovers the "Why" behind behaviors—capturing "unknowns" traditional methods miss—while maintaining strict data security.
Target Audience: Large corporate insights teams and market research agencies requiring bespoke, high-fidelity research.
Disruption: We replace slow, manual moderation with AI-driven agility. Applying our tech in a customized capacity, we offer the speed of a platform with the depth of a consultancy, delivering global insights in days rather than weeks
business_need:
The global insights space suffers from a "Binary Trade-off": brands must choose between the speed/scale of quantitative surveys or the empathy/depth of qualitative interviews. Quantitative data misses the "why," while qualitative research is too slow and expensive for agile, global decision-making.

Converse-in-Q addresses this by providing Qualitative Depth at Quantitative Scale, solving three underserved needs:

The Probing Gap: Traditional surveys are static. If a respondent gives an unexpected answer, the chance to follow up is lost. Our AI bots probe in real-time, capturing nuances previously only available via human moderators.

The Privacy Paradox: Most AI tools risk exposing PII. Our hybrid architecture allows brands to leverage LLMs for insight generation without compromising sensitive consumer data.

Global Agility: We eliminate the "lost in translation" effect, allowing global brands to capture local sentiment in days rather than months.
business_potential:
Originality: We move beyond AI-summarization to AI-native interaction. Our hybrid architecture—physically isolating PII from LLM processing—provides the enterprise-grade security standard GPT-wrappers lack.

Market Potential: We unlock "Qualitative-at-Scale," making deep human nuance affordable for quantitative budgets. We target a massive gap in the research industry where speed usually kills depth.

Scalability: Our tech is cloud-native and multilingual. Example: We recently delivered a KSA tourism project in just one week, capturing and analyzing data across China, Japan, USA, Italy, Germany, and France simultaneously.

Ease of Implementation: As a tech-enabled service, we offer a "zero-learning-curve" model. We manage the technical complexity, providing clients with intuitive KPI dashboards and a "Query Bot" to chat with their data for instant, actionable insights.
case_study:
The Challenge: A leading market research firm required deep consumer sentiment data across six global markets (China, Japan, USA, Italy, Germany, and France) to present at the 2025 Riyadh Tourism Forum. The project demanded qualitative depth but had a critical one-week deadline.

Deployment: We deployed Converse-in-Q to conduct native-language AI interviews. The bot used real-time probing to uncover cultural barriers to travel, while our hybrid architecture kept sensitive respondent PII isolated and secure.

The Result: In just seven days, we delivered a unified global analysis. The client used our Multilingual Query Bot to "chat" with the international dataset, extracting instant localized insights for their forum presentation. We delivered the depth of hundreds of focus groups at the speed of a digital survey, proving that Converse-in-Q can meet the highest stakes in the global insights industry.

name: DoReveal
description:
DoReveal is an AI-powered insights platform that helps researchers uncover deeper understanding from qualitative data. DoReveal prepares and structures datasets to enable more accurate, nuanced and context-aware analysis. This allows the platform to analyze the full dataset and surface both strong trends and softer emerging signals that are often missed.

DoReveal also introduces AI personalization. Users can control the AI’s analytical style and train it on their organization’s voice, frameworks, and tone, ensuring outputs align with how their teams think and communicate insights.

Most importantly, DoReveal is designed to augment, not replace, researchers. Instead of forcing full automation, it fits naturally into existing research workflows, reducing manual analysis while preserving human curiosity, interpretation, and strategic thinking. This enables researchers to move faster from raw data to meaningful insights and business impact.
how_it_works:
Research and insights teams can upload recordings, transcripts, or import activity-based studies from platforms like Recollective and Qualzy. DoReveal provides highly accurate transcriptions and translations from 100+ languages, automatically identifying speakers and distinguishing moderators from participants.

The platform prepares data for AI-assisted analysis, supporting structured grids, thematic analysis, and interactive AI chat. It is trained on frameworks such as Grounded Theory, Emotional Laddering, Personas, Journey Maps, and Jobs-to-Be-Done.

Researchers can quickly find and save verbatim quotes, create audio/video clips from selected text in any language, and build reports seamlessly—making insight generation faster, more accurate, and easier to integrate into existing workflows.

Data remains private and it does not hallucinate.
business_need:
All AI tools naturally offer speed, but DoReveal’s true value lies in the depth and accuracy of analysis. By preparing data and enabling the full dataset to be analyzed—even for large studies with 100+ interviews—DoReveal integrates established analysis frameworks and provides tools that let researchers focus on their craft.

This combination of speed and depth allows researchers to explore far more and uncover richer insights. For example, they can examine multiple perspectives in just minutes, making decisions with greater understanding, nuance, and confidence.
business_potential:
DoReveal is already saving teams over 70% of their analysis time. One customer shared: “It makes us more thorough. It makes us better at what we do. It makes us definitely better, more robust, and more competent.”

While AI is easy to apply, many quick solutions offer speed at the cost of depth. DoReveal’s focus on rigorous, evidence-backed analysis enables any research or insights team—whether an agency or in-house—to generate more business value from their data.

DoReveal is also extremely intuitive. Over 90% of users have never needed training. As one customer put it: “The design is beautiful. It’s very easy to use, intuitive, and fits seamlessly into your normal workflow.”
case_study:
A global B2B advertising brand recently used DoReveal to analyze over 100 customer interviews for a new service offering. By surfacing both dominant trends and subtle signals, DoReveal enabled the team to rigorously interrogate the data and explore insights in depth. Different stakeholders could participate in the process, examining their own perspectives and uncovering opportunities that would have been too costly with traditional methods.

Insights directly informed better client segmentation, optimized messaging, and guided service design, contributing to a 23% revenue increase. DoReveal’s cloud-based platform required no installation, integrated seamlessly into existing workflows, and allowed the team to uncover deeper, higher-impact insights than conventional approaches.

name: Enlightn
description:
Enlightn is a research recruitment engine that turns targeting requirements into matched participant activation through permissioned suppliers, only when the match is strong. The result is higher incidence, fewer screen-outs, and more predictable fieldwork, with an activation log you can actually show.

Most recruitment systems still operate on a “route into screeners” model: send traffic first, then let the screener filter. Enlightn flips that: we match first and activate only high-confidence participants through suppliers. This reduces recruitment waste, improves participant experience (fewer “you don’t qualify” dead ends), and helps suppliers protect their best people.

For insights teams, this means faster time-to-quota, fewer replacements, and clearer provenance, so the data behind decisions is easier to trust and explain. Talking to the right people is the foundation of good insights; we make that step measurable and repeatable.
how_it_works:
Enlightn works as a simple pipeline: Profile => Understand => Match => Activate.

- Profile: build richer participant profiles (open-ended + structured) using a conversational, AI-assisted approach.
- Understand: convert the brief/screener/quotas into clear targeting requirements.
- Match: rank participants by fit, match confidence, and quality signals to identify strong matches.
- Activate: invite only those matches through permissioned supplier systems, then log outcomes to improve future activations and reporting.

This helps buyers reach the right people faster, with fewer replacements and clearer provenance.

Delivery: long-term, this becomes a SaaS buyers can use directly. Today it runs as a managed service (powered by our product) so we can iterate fast and align with client expectations.

Target audience: researchers and research platforms seeking high-quality respondents, and high-quality suppliers looking to monetize their audiences respectfully.
business_need:
Enlightn addresses a trust and efficiency gap between suppliers and buyers in research recruitment.

Suppliers: It’s hard to monetize genuine participants fairly and sustainably. High screen-outs create a poor experience, which drives churn. At the same time, buyers often don’t trust quality claims because they can’t clearly distinguish high-quality vs low-quality sources. Enlightn helps suppliers protect participant experience, and provide buyer-ready proof that supports premium pricing for their best people.

Buyers: They often lack transparency on where respondents come from and don’t have a practical way to tie CPI to quality and outcomes. This makes feasibility unpredictable and “quality” hard to justify internally. Enlightn provides provenance and an audit trail, enabling clearer quality certification and more accountable spend. This is especially important for qual-at-scale: its value depends on reaching engaged participants, and Enlightn helps find them.
business_potential:
Enlightn can change the starting point of sampling. Instead of optimizing recruitment by sending more traffic into screeners, it shifts the system toward match-first activation: selecting who to invite before the screener. That single shift can revamp how sampling is run.

The value isn’t isolated to one stakeholder: it improves outcomes for buyers, suppliers, and participants, and can benefit essentially any company running or supplying research. Buyers get more reliable access to the right people, suppliers can monetize genuine participants more fairly while reducing churn, and participants get a more respectful experience with fewer dead ends.

This can start small with a few suppliers and a few buyers, then expand naturally as integrations deepen and more suppliers adopt match-first activation once they see it protects panelists while supporting premium pricing. Implementation can be lightweight at first, leveraging suppliers’ existing systems and recontact tools.
case_study:
We ran a two-phase pilot to demonstrate “match-first activation” in real conditions.

Phase 1: Profiling (build the matchable pool):
1377 participants entered the profiling flow and 951 completed the profiling survey. The gap is a mix of drop-outs and fraud/low-quality traffic that we terminated in-survey. After cleaning, 821 profiles were tagged as good quality (83.23%). This created a reusable pool of permissioned participants with richer, open-ended + structured profiles that can be re-activated across future studies (instead of re-screening random traffic every time).

Phase 2: Activation (use the pool on a live study):
A buyer needed N=225 completes among recent major appliance buyers (or people actively considering buying one), for a ~10-minute LOI survey at ~$4 CPI. On this study, we observed a 13.5% incidence rate among our profiled respondents, so we were only able to deliver a limited number of completes in this first iteration. The pilot goal wasn’t only volume, it was to validate that match-first activation improves who enters the study (screen-outs + quality), then iterate on scale.

Even with that constraint, when compared to other supplier traffic sources (combined into one benchmark) used in the same context, the Enlightn approach showed a better good-to-bad quality ratio (1.91 vs 1.00) and a lower disqualification rate (14% vs 34%).

How it’s deployed / what the buyer receives:
Buyer shares brief/screener/quotas => Enlightn produces a match-based activation plan => suppliers activate via their existing recontact tools => buyer receives an activation log (provenance + outcomes) and a pilot scorecard (incidence, disqualification, completion, drop-off). This proof layer helps teams tie spend to outcomes and compare sources more objectively.

We identified multiple concrete opportunities to improve incidence and scale delivery further (not all can be shared publicly).

name: Facilitated Storytelling & "5 Variables of Emotional Meaning" Framework
description:
Studies show that 95% of human decision-making happens in the non-conscious mind; only 5% occurs in conscious awareness. Traditional qualitative methods that use facilitated dialogue can explain the 5%, but not the 95%. This means brands are making major decisions based on a fraction of consumer understanding.

We believe the telling of life stories unlocks the non-conscious narratives that truly drive consumer behavior. Hidden in those narratives are the emotional truths consumers seek to validate through the brands they purchase. When a brand reflects those truths back, it moves beyond “this applies to me” to reach “this feels like me.” 

Our facilitated storytelling process engages consumers in telling the life stories that surface the non-conscious emotional truths most relevant to a given brand. Using proprietary AI, we code those truths into our “5 Variables of Emotional Meaning” framework, enabling us to write our narrative insights that reveal what traditional methods miss.
how_it_works:
Our target audience is insight teams in consumer goods and services. We are a qualitative partner built for strategic activation, by brand and agency strategists.

Our self-guided storytelling technology requires no human-to-respondent interaction. We deliver results as quickly and affordably as our competitors, while providing narrative insights that reveal a deeper understanding of consumer behavior than traditional insights.

Our coding framework allows brands to quickly identify which variables are needed to solve strategic issues. Brands can easily turn emotional meaning into communications that achieve emotional resonance.

•	Beliefs – Non-conscious truths that shape what a consumer thinks and does.
•	Attitudes – How the consumer lives their beliefs in everyday life.
•	Behaviors – The beliefs and attitudes combine to drive behaviors.
•	Benefits to Target – How those behaviors benefit the target.
•	Benefits to Others – How those behaviors benefit people in the target’s life.
business_need:
Our methodology is designed to solve very specific needs.

1.	Finding the “why”: Traditional facilitated dialogue often confuses a “brand why” with a “category why.” Our facilitated storytelling uncovers consumers’ non-conscious emotional truths, helping brands define a why that is truly differentiating and ownable. This elevates the brand’s why to a relevant factor in driving purchase.
2.	Closing the “say-do” gap: Brands struggle when consumers say one thing but do another. Because our methodology reveals the 95%, we explain that disconnect and close the gap.
3.	Closing the “results-to-activation” gap: Our coding framework translates findings into clear, strategic action.
4.	Thinking global, acting local: Think global means aligning behind a set of shared beliefs while act local means understanding the attitudes that are unique to each market.
business_potential:
ORIGINALITY: The neurological processes unique to storytelling make our methodology original. Telling life stories bypasses the conscious analytical processes that judge responses as right or wrong, allowing consumers’ non-conscious truths to be expressed.

MARKET POTENTIAL: The total addressable market for research and analytics in the US is $16.4 billion (based on a 5-year average 2020 – 2024). Qualitative research accounts for about 18% of that for a serviceable available market of $3 billion. 

IMPLEMENTATION: In April, we will finish automating our process to remove 80% of the human hours it takes to execute a project. Clients want to know humans are part of the process; our goal is to keep that around 15 hours so we maintain a 70% margin.

SCALABILITY: As adoption of AI-moderated research grows, brands will still need deeper qualitative research to have a complete understanding of consumer behavior. This is how our methodology fills a need and what gives us large scale potential.
case_study:
BUSINESS PROBLEM: Hasbro needed to increase board game purchases among moderate / light households. Their traditional research suggested reminding parents about board games would drive sales, yet behavior didn’t change, a classic “say-do” gap. The solution came from understanding why heavy households play (not just buy) more often.

STORYTELLING: Parents told a story about a recent family-time experience, then answered probing questions that were analyzed and coded into the five variables.

FINDINGS: Two attitudes explained the difference in behaviors.
1.	Definition of quality time: Moderate/light parents define quality time as simply being together, even without interaction; heavy households require active engagement.
2.	Scheduling quality time: Moderate/light parents don’t intentionally schedule quality time while heavy parents do.

RECOMMENDATION: Build a communication platform that reshapes moderate/light parents’ definition and planning of quality time to mirror that of heavy households.

NARRATIVE INSIGHT: “I BELIEVE, as a parent, I’m responsible for creating a strong family unit. So, when it comes to quality time with my family, my ATTITUDE is, the activities must focus on the family members interacting with each other. I BEHAVE by intentionally scheduling these activities for my family. This BENEFITS ME by fulfilling my responsibility as a parent and it BENEFITS MY FAMILY by helping them learn about each other, so they build stronger relationships.”

name: Insight-Lab, AI Ecosystem
description:
Insight-lab is a one-stop AI platform built specifically for market research and insights.
It combines a RAG-powered knowledge vault with citation traceability, multi-LLM orchestration, a no-code agent builder, synthetic persona simulation, and an insight studio that produces executive-ready outputs in minutes.
Unlike generic AI tools, this platform was designed around the real workflow of research teams. It turns institutional knowledge into a living, searchable memory layer. It allows teams to deploy specialized AI agents tied to specific projects. And it enables secure client interaction without losing governance or control.
The result is not another chatbot. It is infrastructure for insight generation, activation, and delivery.
how_it_works:
Insight-lab supports the full research lifecycle from study design to executive delivery inside one unified environment.
Teams collaborate in shared workspaces with role-based permissions. Researchers, analysts, and strategists work together on the same project designing methodology, defining samples, drafting instruments, interpreting statistical outputs, analyzing qualitative and quantitative data, generating synthetic datasets, and building final insight narratives.
All materials are embedded into encrypted vector knowledge bases with citation-backed responses and complete audit traceability.
Clients receive a secure external view where they can speak directly with a project-trained AI Agent. They can explore findings, ask follow-up questions, and test scenarios in real time. This removes friction, reduces repetitive agency back and forth, and transforms reporting into interactive decision support.
The platform is API-first and MCP-ready, enabling seamless enterprise integration
business_need:
The insights industry has a memory problem and a delivery problem.
Research accumulates but becomes hard to reuse. Reports live in drives. Knowledge disappears when teams change. Organizations end up paying twice for what they already learned.
At the same time, delivery is still static. A presentation cannot answer new questions. Stakeholders depend on the agency to interpret every follow-up.
Insight-lab addresses both gaps. It converts research into a structured, searchable AI memory layer with full traceability. It also turns insight delivery into an interactive experience where clients can engage directly with project-trained AI agents while maintaining security and governance.
business_potential:
Insight-lab introduces a new category: AI infrastructure for research operations.
Originality comes from integration. RAG memory architecture, multi-LLM orchestration, synthetic persona modeling, agent publishing, client-facing AI interfaces, and governance controls operate inside one platform built specifically for insights teams.
Market potential is strong because every agency and enterprise research team faces data fragmentation, slow reporting cycles, and unmanaged AI experimentation.
The platform is cloud-native, modular, and API-driven, which makes it scalable across regions and organizations.
Implementation is straightforward. Teams upload existing research, configure agents without coding, and begin delivering interactive AI-powered insights almost immediately.
case_study:
3 Years of Knowledge, Activated in 3 Days
Research Agency  is a 20-person US research agency serving CPG and retail brands. Over three years, they completed 250+ studies stored across Drive, SharePoint, decks, spreadsheets, and transcripts. When a client asks, “What do Gen Z consumers think about sustainable packaging?”, analysts spend days manually searching old files.

Day 1 — Knowledge Activation
The team creates a secure workspace in Insight-lab and bulk-uploads historical studies. The platform extracts content, structures metadata, and builds a searchable AI knowledge layer with citation-backed traceability. Within hours, three years of research becomes instantly accessible.

Day 2 — Agent Creation
A strategist builds a Brand Health Specialist agent connected to tracking studies and a synthetic Gen Z persona grounded in prior research segments. Using Studio tools, the team generates an executive briefing, thematic synthesis, and visual summary in hours instead of a week.

Day 3 — Client Delivery
Instead of sending a static deck, the agency shares a secure AI interface with the client’s CMO. The CMO asks follow-up questions directly in chat and receives data-backed answers tied to specific studies. Engagement analytics show time spent and topics explored.

Research retrieval drops from days to seconds. Reporting cycles shrink dramatically. Knowledge becomes permanent. Insight delivery becomes interactive.

Insight-lab runs as secure cloud SaaS with role-based permissions, server-side multi-LLM orchestration, and API integration. Currently in pilot with US research teams modernizing their workflow.

name: Insights Integrity Initiative
description:
The insights ecosystem is under structural strain: declining respondent experience, rising low-quality participation, and the emergence of agentic AI that will further strain traditional fraud defenses.
The Insights Integrity Initiative introduces a governed Respondent Continuity Engine, an independent longitudinal layer designed to restore structural integrity across the ecosystem.  Panelists complete short, rotating profiling and fraud instruments outside of client surveys.  This enables demographic enrichment via API, longitudinal coherence analysis per panelist, improved respondent experience through shorter LOIs, and stronger defenses against AI-generated participation.  
By maintaining persistent identity and evaluating cross-survey behavior over time, the initiative strengthens data integrity at its structural foundation.  Additionally, we put agentic AI in conflicting surveys to measure consistency.  Without that, agentic AI will become invisible in the near future.
how_it_works:
The initiative operates through an independent Respondent Continuity Engine governed by an industry committee. Participating sample providers route panelists through short, cadenced profiling and fraud instruments hosted via survey platforms. These instruments assess longitudinal coherence rather than single-survey validity.
The Engine maintains persistent respondent identity and analyzes cross-survey behavior over time. Sample providers receive stewardship diagnostics to strengthen panel quality. Research buyers access verified demographic enrichment via API, reducing redundant survey questions and improving respondent experience.
Unlike traditional fraud tools, this is not a per-survey detection service. It is a governed continuity layer designed for sample providers, platforms, and research buyers seeking structural quality improvement across the ecosystem.
Survey data is not collected on client projects.  Rather, the profiling surveys serve as the fraud detection tools.
business_need:
The global insights ecosystem lacks a shared longitudinal continuity layer. Respondent identity, profiling, and quality validation operate transactionally and independently across providers, platforms, and studies. Profiling questions are repeatedly asked, quality checks are isolated within individual surveys, and panel stewardship is fragmented. This structure creates redundancy, respondent fatigue, inefficiency, and systemic blind spots that fraud exploits.
Existing solutions focus on detecting low-quality responses within single surveys rather than repairing the underlying discontinuity across the ecosystem. The unmet need is structural. The Insights Integrity Initiative addresses this by introducing governed, longitudinal respondent continuity that strengthens participation standards and elevates data integrity across the industry.
business_potential:
The initiative is original in that it reframes fraud and low-quality participation as a structural discontinuity problem rather than a per-survey detection problem. It does not compete with existing fraud tools; it coordinates and elevates them within a governed continuity framework.
Market potential is ecosystem-wide. Sample providers gain stronger panel stewardship, survey platforms gain differentiated enrichment capabilities, and research buyers reduce redundancy while receiving higher-quality data. The API-based enrichment model supports sustainable funding for governance and audit functions without centralizing power.
The architecture is scalable across providers and geographies and can be implemented incrementally through pilot participation before broader adoption, reducing friction to entry.
case_study:
A global brand commissions a multi-country tracking study through a participating sample provider and survey platform.
Instead of repeating extensive demographic and profiling grids within the survey, the platform calls the Respondent Continuity Engine API to retrieve verified profiling attributes maintained within the Respondent Continuity Engine. The survey instrument is shorter, reducing respondent fatigue and mid-survey dropout while preserving targeting accuracy.
Separately, participating sample providers route panelists through short, rotating profiling and fraud instruments on a regular cadence outside of client surveys. These instruments are designed by the independent governance committee and assess longitudinal coherence rather than single-survey validity.
The Continuity Engine maintains persistent respondent identity and evaluates cross-survey behavior over time. Because fraud often relies on inconsistent identity, shifting digital fingerprints, or narrative instability, longitudinal analysis exposes patterns that isolated survey checks miss. When coherence degrades beyond expected thresholds, the sample provider receives stewardship diagnostics to determine appropriate action internally. Research buyers never receive fraud labels; they receive enriched profiling data only.
Over time, the ecosystem benefits from reduced redundancy, fewer post-field removals, improved respondent experience, and earlier detection of low-quality and AI-generated participation.
The initiative is currently seeking founding ecosystem partners to pilot this framework in controlled deployments across select providers and platforms.

name: Lifemind.ai

Lifemind.ai segments customers without any PII to show which personal value groups purchase the most from a brand while connecting the insights directly to creative content and targeting - in minutes. Lifemind.ai aligns marketing to what customers value the most to create far greater product-market fit.

Lifemind.ai also enables to talk to customer segments using virtual focus groups to test ideas, develop new product ideas, and more. Lifemind.ai disrupts the current landscape for customer segmentation, which is typically based on demographics and behaviors that have been measured for decades. Current solutions also take weeks or months and struggle to connect to targeting. 

Lifemind.ai brings new insights to marketers to reignite growth while delivering actionable creative and targeting assets in minutes.

business_potential:
No other solution provides the insights Lifemind.ai has unless you execute customized research, which takes months and is rarely actionable. 

Lifemind.ai can be implemented within minutes and can be used by a broad spectrum of marketing managers and agency personnel.
case_study:

Within 30 minutes, on the day a brand signs up with Lifemind.ai, a marketing manager can establish their brand on Lifemind.ai, upload a customer report for a specific customer group, view segmentation with personas for every segment, chat with the highest ranking segments, generate creative content for any segment, and generate targeting files for any segment. 

The result is fresh, new, powerful insights tied to high-ranking segments that creative and media teams can use immediately to reignite growth in acquisition, retention, and loyalty. No special expertise required. No PII.

name: Lisa Vissichelli
description:
Data Vissichelli is a design consultancy that transforms complex research into compelling visual stories. Researchers uncover powerful insights, yet dense reports often fail to resonate. In an industry saturated with dashboards and templated outputs, our innovation lies in custom-crafted visual storytelling — using illustration, infographics, and accessible design to help people feel and understand insights, not just read them.

We partner with research and strategy teams to interpret findings and translate quantitative, qualitative, and behavioral data into empathy-driven journey maps, personas, executive summaries, and strategic reports that drive alignment.

Led by award-winning data visualization expert Lisa Vissichelli, recognized by Adobe and the Information is Beautiful Awards, we distill complex information into memorable visual narratives that decision-makers share and act on.
how_it_works: Our solution operates as a rapid, high-impact visual storytelling service embedded within existing research workflows. We partner with insight and strategy teams to interpret findings and translate quantitative, qualitative, and behavioral data into custom-crafted visual experiences. This includes illustrated journeys, empathy-driven personas, executive infographics and strategic reports. Because we work from completed research, implementation is fast and seamless. Clients see strong ROI through reduced internal design time, faster stakeholder alignment, and greater impact from existing research investments. Our primary audience includes enterprise insight teams, research agencies, healthcare organizations, and strategy leaders who need complex consumer attitudes and behaviors to resonate across large, cross-functional groups. By transforming insights into accessible, emotionally engaging visual narratives, we enable research to be understood, remembered, and acted upon.
business_need:
Researchers uncover remarkable insights, but translating dense data into clear, compelling visuals remains a challenge. Data Vissichelli addresses this gap by centering insight delivery on human understanding. We help research teams turn their unique findings into custom-crafted visual stories that resonate with any audience — from executives to consumers.

Through illustration, infographics, and accessible design, we transform complex reports into human-centered narratives that are easier to understand, remember, and act on. By focusing on how insights are experienced, not just delivered, we ensure research is inclusive, emotionally engaging, and truly understood — closing the gap between knowledge and action.
business_potential:
Originality: Data Vissichelli introduces an innovative creative storytelling approach to insights. We position design, illustration, and narrative as essential tools for insight interpretation and empathy-building, rather than decorative outputs.
Market Potential: As insight teams struggle with stakeholder engagement and insight fatigue, demand is growing for differentiated, high-quality visual storytelling that resonates. This represents an untapped opportunity within the insights ecosystem, particularly for organizations seeking more human, inclusive communication of research.
Scalability: While each solution is custom crafted, the underlying storytelling approach scales across industries, audiences, and research types. The model supports repeat engagement, enterprise partnerships, and integration into ongoing insight programs.
Ease of Implementation: Our solution integrates seamlessly into existing research workflows, requiring no new platforms or tools. By working directly with exi
case_study:
In a complex oncology study exploring patient journeys, the research team partnered with Data Vissichelli to bring raw, text-based insights to life. The team’s goal was to better understand patient experiences in an environment that is challenging to diagnose and treat, and to communicate these findings in a way that could drive empathy, alignment, and action among pharmaceutical stakeholders and healthcare teams.

We began by consulting with the client to understand their objectives, intended audiences, and use cases for the report. Together, we identified the most critical insights to highlight,  from patient triggers and treatment touchpoints to emotional and behavioral patterns, and discussed how visuals could be used to make these insights immediately understandable and actionable.

I then proposed a design solution for a 40-slide report, focusing on building empathy and clarity while speaking the client’s language. Each visualization was custom-crafted, from illustrated patient journeys and storyboards to accessible infographics and detailed diagrams, ensuring that no element was generic. The design emphasized human-centered storytelling, helping stakeholders not just see, but experience the patient journey.

Once the client approved the proposals, the report was fully visualized and completed in just two days. The result was a highly engaging, empathy-building tool that enabled researchers and pharmaceutical stakeholders to quickly grasp complex patient experiences, align on strategies, strengthen physician relationships, and develop actionable steps that directly supported patient care. By transforming dense data into human-centered visual narratives, the project ensured the insights were not just presented, but truly understood, remembered, and acted upon.

name: Mona
description: Traditional surveys force people into checkboxes. The result is low response rates, shallow data, and the people closest to the work are left unheard. Deskless workers, multilingual teams, frontline staff. Lisa replaces surveys with adaptive AI conversations. Respondents text or talk naturally, in any of 30+ languages, from their phone. No app, no login, no laptop. Lisa listens and follows up. If someone mentions their manager, Lisa digs deeper. If they raise workload, Lisa explores that. There is no fixed script. Completion takes a fraction of the time compared to traditional suveys. The real innovation is what this unlocks at scale: hundreds of rich qualitative conversations, analyzed automatically to surface themes and patterns. Lisa is a conversational AI insight discovery platform. Research technology, not HR software. Employee experience is our first market, but the methodology applies anywhere you need to understand what people think. Consumer research, CX, brand perception.
how_it_works: The user describes what they want to learn, and Lisa's AI wizard generates a complete conversational guide in seconds. They can also select topics manually. Either way they get editable dialogue flows, ready to launch immediately. They choose how Lisa modarates: script mode that follows the guide exactly, or adaptive AI mode that follows up naturally based on what respondents say. Preview it, share a link. Respondents tap the link on any phone. No app, no login, no portal. Lisa conducts a conversation in their language (30+ supported), adapting questions in real time. It feels like texting a colleague. As responses flow in, AI analyzes qualitative data at scale, surfacing themes, patterns, and insights in real time across hundreds of conversations. The disruption is qualitative insight without the qualitative infrastructure. No survey design expertise, no panel recruitment, no weeks of fieldwork.An operations manager can go from question to insight in hours, completely self-serve.
business_need: Across every research context, whether employee, consumer, or CX, the same problem kills insight quality: people don't say what they actually think. 39% of employees are uncomfortable giving honest feedback. 60% of burnout drivers are invisible in traditional surveys. The number one predictor of attrition, toxic workplace behavior, doesn't show up in checkbox data. This is not a survey design problem. It is a methodology failure. Structured questionnaires capture symptoms but miss root causes ("I'm excluded from decisions"). The result is that organizations act on scores without context, and $8.9 trillion in global productivity is lost annually. The gap widens for hard-to-reach populations. Deskless workers, multilingual teams, frontline staff. These are the people closest to operational reality, and they are the least likely to complete a 15-minute survey on a laptop they don't have. Traditional research gets 8-15% response rates from these groups.
business_potential: Lisa is not a better survey. It's a new methodology. Adaptive AI conversations replaxe fixed questionnaires, discovering what respondents want to say rather than limiting them to predetermined answers. Employee engagement alone is a $1.3B+ market, and 2.7 billion deskless workers globally are underserved by every existing tool. But employee insights is just our entry point. The same conversational methodology applies directly to consumer research, CX, and brand perception.Platform, not a service. AI handles both moderation and analysis, so no researchers are needed. 30+ languages, automatic theme extraction, link-based distribution. A client scales from 50 to 5,000 respondents without changing their workflow or adding headcount. So it is, originality, market potential, scalability and ease of implementation
case_study:
1) The hospitality group with seasonal churn
Company: 600 employees across 8 hotel/restaurant locations. Multilingual workforce.
Trigger: Half of seasonal hires leave within 6 weeks. Cost: €3,200 per failed hire, hundreds of times a year.
How Mona enters: "Run a check-in conversation at week 2 and week 4 with every new hire. In 5 languages."
What surfaces: Training is too fast. Night shifts feel abandoned. Team leads don’t know names. Job ads don’t match reality.
Result: Onboarding extended. Buddy system for night shifts. Job ads rewritten. Early attrition drops 35%

2) The logistics company with a turnover spike
Company: 250 employees, 3 distribution hubs. HR is one person + a recruiter.
Trigger: Driver churn is climbing. Exit surveys get 12% response. Q3 numbers are getting worse.
How Mona enters: "Run a 2-week conversation with your drivers. 3 minutes, on their phone, anonymous."
What surfaces: Routing feels punishing. Breaks are chaotic. Equipment issues go unreported. Small things matter most.
Result: Route rotation implemented. Scanners repaired. Feedback channel opened. Turnover drops 22% in 90 days.

name: N-Infinite by Livepanel
description:
N-Infinite is the market's first ML-driven data augmentation application. Powered by a proprietary 3-layer algorithm, it seamlessly amplifies data from any quantitative or mixed-methods survey. It supports all question formats—including open-ended verbatims and visual stimuli analysis—augmenting specific segments or full samples from 20% to over 300%.

Beyond traditional research, it is the preferred solution for digital twin companies to enhance their training data quality, and for enterprise brands scaling marketing insights across their entire customer universe.

Highly versatile, N-Infinite offers flexible deployment via SaaS, API, custom plugins, or consultative managed services
how_it_works:
N-Infinite is powered by a proprietary algorithm that dynamically generates between 10 and 250 machine learning models for every survey question—or even individual response options. Evaluating strict performance metrics, it selects the optimal model to execute a 3-stage sequential prediction for un-surveyed profiles.

Stage 1 guarantees strict adherence to the survey's internal routing logic and consistency. Stage 2 accurately predicts the responses. Stage 3 leverages our SmartMatch technology to ensure precise sample representativeness across all categories.

Beyond predictive modeling, the platform provides an end-to-end suite for data preprocessing, feature engineering, anomaly detection, post-processing, and visualization.

This pipeline is highly automated, robust, and massively scalable. Crucially, the system processes zero PII (Personally Identifiable Information), ensuring full compliance with stringent global data privacy standards.
business_need:
N-Infinite solves the classic fieldwork dilemma: missing deadlines and budgets due to hard-to-reach audiences and strict quotas. While programmatic sampling handles general feasibility quickly, fulfilling those final, complex quotas often takes weeks or forces researchers to relax sample composition.

While not a substitute for massive raw samples, N-Infinite enables robust subgroup drill-downs without the base size (n) collapsing, delivered in minutes at a fraction of the cost. It is a game-changer for B2B research, where qualified incidence rates (IR) are low and cost-per-interview (CPI) is exorbitant.

Furthermore, independent validations prove it acts as a premier data quality tool: augmenting strictly vetted, high-quality completes yields sharper insights than accepting sub-par panel data just to hit quotas.

Finally, it empowers Digital Twins and Enterprise CX. By bridging the gap between robust transactional databases and scarce declarative data (often from <1% response rates),
business_potential:
N-Infinite is not a theoretical promise; it is a proven reality with 5,000+ globally delivered projects.

Originality: We pioneered ML-driven data augmentation specifically for insights, moving beyond traditional weighting to accurately synthesize declarative data without requiring PII.

Market Potential: 20-30% of all MRx studies face severe feasibility constraints (low IRs/hard quotas) that we directly solve. Beyond agencies, we have validated demand from Digital Twin developers to enrich training models. However, our largest long-term TAM lies in Enterprise CX: bridging the gap for corporations that possess massive transactional databases but face severe declarative data scarcity.

Scalability & Implementation: Built on a highly automated ML pipeline, N-Infinite scales effortlessly. It is seamlessly deployed today via SaaS, API, and plugins by 50+ global clients, ensuring frictionless adoption and immediate ROI.
case_study:
Rather than hypothetical scenarios, N-Infinite is actively deployed across various methodologies. Here are three real-world case studies:

Case Study 1: Hard-to-Reach Quotas (Socio-Educational Polling, Mexico)

Challenge: A client struggled to fulfill low SES (Socioeconomic Status D/E) quotas. Fieldwork dragged on for over a month against a rigid deadline.

Solution: Leveraging partial raw completes and our micro-census data variant, N-Infinite synthesized the remaining strict quotas, closing the project in under 24 hours.

Case Study 2: Enterprise CX & Segmentation (Leading Retailer)

Challenge: Planning an overhaul of its non-bank credit services, the retailer surveyed its CRM base but hit a <2% response rate.

Solution: N-Infinite ingested this scarce declarative data alongside rich transactional and onboarding records. It accurately profiled their entire customer universe, enabling precise cohort segmentation that drove a 200% ROI increase on targeted campaigns.

Case Study 3: Multi-Market Tracker (Global B2B Client)

Challenge: An annual 12-market Food & Beverage tracker required large base sizes. The client faced scarce B2B panelist availability and exorbitant CPIs (Cost Per Interview).

Solution: Utilizing rich firmographic data from previous waves, N-Infinite augmented the dataset. The client achieved their goals with only 50% of the usual fieldwork, slashing costs by 30% and accelerating delivery times by 80%.

name: Nichefire
description: Nichefire is a predictive cultural discovery platform that helps enterprises see what’s coming next in consumer behavior before the market reacts. Using AI to detect and model emerging cultural signals, Nichefire surfaces which trends are accelerating, why they matter, and how to act on them 12–18 months ahead. Brands like Walmart, Nestlé, and Kraft Heinz use Nichefire to de-risk innovation, sharpen strategy, and move faster without relying on slow, expensive traditional research.
how_it_works:
Nichefire works by continuously scanning and modeling cultural signals across social, search, and media to identify emerging shifts in consumer attitudes and behaviors before they become obvious. Instead of requiring predefined questions or keywords, it surfaces early narratives, tracks their momentum, and translates them into clear implications for product, brand, and strategy teams. This helps brands understand not just what consumers are saying today, but what they are beginning to expect next.

The platform is built for large enterprise teams across insights, marketing, innovation, and strategy, enabling shared, decision-ready foresight without reliance on panels or slow research cycles. Unlike traditional services or static trend reports, Nichefire is an always-on, self-serve platform that delivers faster, predictive insights that were previously unattainable through conventional methods.
business_need:
Nichefire addresses a major unmet need in the global insights space: the ability to anticipate cultural and consumer change before it is visible, measurable, or already priced into decisions. Most insights tools and research methods are reactive by design. They rely on known questions, historical data, panels, or surveys that confirm what has already happened, often months too late to create real competitive advantage.

What’s underserved is early-stage discovery of emerging attitudes, behaviors, and expectations the “unknown unknowns” that shape demand, risk, and relevance next. Teams know culture matters, but they lack confidence in which signals deserve action, which are noise, and how to translate early signals into business decisions.

Nichefire fills this gap by detecting and forecasting cultural shifts before they mainstream, giving enterprises a predictive layer of insight that traditional research, social listening, and consulting models cannot deliver at speed or scale.
business_potential: Nichefire’s business potential is driven by a clear gap in the global insights market: enterprises need predictive, not reactive, understanding of culture to compete. The concept is original because it focuses on uncovering unknown emerging signals before brands know what to ask, rather than analyzing predefined topics or historical trends. Market potential is significant across CPG, retail, QSR, finance, and healthcare, where faster insight directly impacts revenue, innovation success, and risk mitigation. The platform is highly scalable as an always-on, AI-powered SaaS solution that serves large cross-functional teams without incremental consulting costs. Implementation is easy because Nichefire is self-serve, integrates with existing insight stacks, and delivers decision-ready outputs quickly, enabling value in weeks rather than months.
case_study:
Nestlé used Nichefire to understand how emerging health behaviors tied to GLP-1 weight loss drugs would impact food demand. Rather than commissioning months of traditional research, Nestlé deployed Nichefire as an always-on platform for its consumer insights and innovation teams.

Nichefire surfaced an early cultural shift showing that GLP-1 users were not just eating less, but redefining what “appropriate” meals looked like smaller portions, high-protein, nutrient-dense foods that still felt satisfying. At the time, this signal had not yet appeared in mainstream research or panel data.

Using Nichefire’s predictive modeling, Nestlé identified clear opportunity spaces months ahead of competitors, informing product formulation, positioning, and portfolio strategy. This foresight directly contributed to the launch and expansion of the Vital Pursuit line, representing an estimated $150M+ revenue opportunity.

In addition to revenue impact, Nestlé reduced trend discovery and synthesis time by up to 70% across 120+ stakeholders, accelerating decision-making and alignment across insights, innovation, and marketing teams. Nichefire was deployed as a self-serve SaaS platform, integrating into existing workflows and enabling continuous foresight rather than one-off studies.

name: Opinio Echo
description:
Opinio Echo is a Reviews Intelligence Layer that transforms public reviews into structured conversations, recommendations, and competitive intelligence. It goes beyond analysis by helping customers write better reviews, upgrading the ecosystem at its source.
Customers share feedback through Voice Mode (AI structures spoken input), AI-Crafted Mode (SEO-optimized suggestions), or Conversational Mode (AI probes for clarity). The result is richer, clearer feedback with minimal effort and costs.
Using AI-driven ingestion, probing, and theme extraction, Echo converts shallow reviews into structured, SEO-ready content and research-grade insights. It identifies experience drivers, pain points, behavioral signals, and competitor gaps in real time, improving interpretation, benchmarking, and prioritization.
Its dual impact elevates review quality while transforming public feedback into continuous, research-grade intelligence, bridging social listening and formal market research.
how_it_works:
Opinio Echo ingests publicly available Reviews and structures them using AI-powered NLP. It clusters themes, analyzes sentiment intensity, detects experience drivers, and generates clear improvement recommendations. 
On the customer side, it empowers customers to give better feedback. Through Voice, AI-Crafted, and Conversational modes, it helps individuals articulate richer, clearer feedback, suggests SEO-optimized review lines, and converts discussions into structured Google reviews. This elevates feedback quality before it even reaches the public domain.
Businesses receive dashboards with theme summaries, user-level diagnostics, competitor benchmarking, and actionable insights.
Echo is a scalable SaaS platform, not a manual service, designed for SMEs, multi-location brands, CX teams, and agencies. It delivers continuous, faster, always-on intelligence using existing public data, unlocking insights previously buried in fragmented reviews.
business_need:
Globally, brands collect vast volumes of unsolicited public feedback across platforms like Google Reviews, TripAdvisor, and Yelp, yet struggle to convert it into structured, decision-ready intelligence. Reviews are emotionally rich but unstructured, brief, and inconsistent, while most tools focus only on sentiment scoring and keyword tracking, offering limited diagnostic depth.
Parallelly, businesses invest heavily in surveys to understand experience gaps, despite already possessing real-time customer feedback in the public domain.
There is also a quality gap: customers often leave short reviews that limit both SEO impact and insight depth.
Opinio Echo addresses this need by enhancing feedback quality through AI-guided conversational probing, converting shallow reviews into structured, SEO-optimized content, and transforming public feedback into driver-level analysis, competitor benchmarking, and recommendations.
Echo creates a continuous, affordable, always-on intelligence for brands.
business_potential:
Opinio Echo introduces a new category: review enhancement plus intelligence activation. Its originality lies in improving feedback quality while simultaneously converting it into structured competitive insights.

Market potential is significant, millions of businesses rely on Google Reviews for visibility and reputation management, yet lack diagnostic intelligence tools. Echo serves SMEs, enterprises, agencies, and multi-location brands globally.

The platform is highly scalable, AI-driven, and SaaS-based, requiring no new data collection. Implementation is simple: businesses leverage existing public reviews, lowering adoption barriers.

Its recurring subscription model, vertical customization (healthcare, hospitality, retail, education), and potential API integrations position Echo as a scalable, high-growth solution in the global insights and CX ecosystem.
case_study:
A regional fashion retailer operating 15 stores relied on Google Review QR codes printed on receipts to collect customer feedback. While ratings averaged 4.2 stars, brand struggled to extract insights from hundreds of brief reviews such as “nice collection” or “slow checkout.” Star ratings provided surface signals but lacked clarity on the experience drivers influencing satisfaction and repeat visits.
Opinio Echo was deployed as an intelligent layer over the retailer’s existing QR system. Instead of directing customers immediately to Google, the QR redirected to Echo’s mobile-first interface. Customers could speak feedback naturally (Voice Mode), select AI-crafted suggestions, or engage in a short conversational flow with AI driven probing for contextual clarification. The interaction remained frictionless and optional. Once finalized, reviews were submitted to Google.
During the pilot, all three modes were used consistently- Voice for convenience, AI-Crafted for speed, and Conversational for more nuanced experiences. As review depth increased, previously hidden drivers surfaced. Instead of generic “slow checkout,” customers described confusion during loyalty redemption. Rather than “unhelpful staff,” reviews referenced unclear return policy explanations.
Echo structured enriched reviews into recurring themes across locations. Leadership discovered that underperforming stores were not facing staffing shortages, as assumed, but inconsistencies in policy communication. Competitor benchmarking showed nearby outlets were praised more for clarity than operational speed.
Without launching additional surveys, the retailer converted its existing review ecosystem into a continuous insight stream. The model is replicable across industries and franchise networks through existing QR code system, website review buttons, post-purchase email or SMS links, CRM triggers, or direct Google Business integrations, without requiring changes to operational systems.

name: Org Brain
description:
AI is already inside work — connected to Notion, Slack, Salesforce, and dozens of tools. The adoption problem is solved. And the results are getting worse.
Not because AI is failing — because AI is succeeding without coherence. Connect AI to everything and it pulls from outdated strategy decks, unapproved drafts, and contradictory analyses — producing confident answers that reflect none accurately.
The problem isn't access. It's classification. Business knowledge has no universal structure, so AI retrieves candidates instead of canon.
Starling Workspace gives every organization an AI Chief of Staff that works from authoritative knowledge, not search results. Users build a structured Org Brain through guided conversation — no code, no technical setup. Every piece of knowledge gets a permanent semantic address. The AI loads canonical sources directly instead of searching.
$99/month. LLM-agnostic. Sovereign data. Switch providers without losing a single memory.
data_modalities: Text; Digital/Behavioral; Social/Cultural; No New Data
how_it_works:
Semantic addressing replaces search with permanent coordinates that resolve instantly regardless of scale. MA20 means Competitive Position everywhere, for any AI. No vector search. No disambiguation.
Three-layer stack: Org Brain (Notion) stores canonical knowledge, client-owned. MCP Server bridges Org Brain to any LLM. The LLM is stateless — full context in, nothing retained.
Users build their Org Brain through guided conversational installers the platform routes automatically. One session produces six memory nodes in under an hour. At 23 memories, the AI Chief of Staff activates intelligence mode — cross-domain synthesis, drift detection, autonomous insight generation.
LLM-agnostic — switch providers without losing context. The platform is fully self-serve and what we're scaling.
business_need:
Lean made manufacturing efficient. Design Thinking made products human-centered. Neither was built for a world where AI is inside every workflow, generating at scale, operating without organizational context.
Industry 5.0 demands a new design methodology. Not for products or processes, but for how organizations think. We call it Cognitive Design.
Cognitive Design maps organizational intelligence across two hemispheres and a processing core. The Innovation hemisphere codifies why you exist: identity, market position, innovation, brand. The Optimization hemisphere codifies how you execute: productivity, workflows, processes, culture. The Cognitive Core bridges both with measurement and integration that unify the whole brain.
The insight industry needs this. Research firms sit on decades of knowledge with no architecture underneath. Cognitive Design structures that knowledge so AI can reason from it. Starling Workspace delivers it as a self-serve platform.
business_potential:
Originality: No platform solves the classification problem for business knowledge. Current solutions improve search (RAG, embeddings). Starling Workspace eliminates search — permanent coordinates resolve to canonical sources in constant time.
Market: Enterprise AI platforms are $115B in 2026, reaching $273B by 2031. The technology works — the architecture doesn't. AI is inside work but can't distinguish canon from commentary. What Lean did for manufacturing and Design Thinking did for product, Starling Workspace does for organizational intelligence, through guided conversation. $99/month + cost-plus AI usage.
Scalability: Retrieval stays flat as knowledge grows — O(1) vs O(n). Sovereign design — the Org Brain is your asset, the LLM is the commodity. Switch providers without losing context. At 23 memories, intelligence mode activates autonomously.
Implementation: Natural conversation, no code. One session creates six memory nodes in under an hour.
case_study:
Brady Patterson, COO of The Truffle Box, put Starling's Org Brain to two distinct uses — proving the architecture transfers across business and personal contexts.
First, he ran the full installer sequence for The Truffle Box, his wife Jamie's hypnotherapy business. Through guided conversation, he built the Org Brain from scratch — codifying values, market position, brand, and operations into semantically addressed memory nodes. Jamie completed all 28 Constitutional memories, activating a fully autonomous AI Chief of Staff. They have Smart OS active and are learning to engage with it. Brady built it. She runs it.
Then he adapted the architecture into BradyOS — a personal operating system. After a car accident left both managing cognitive recovery, Brady had 14 years of knowledge across five platforms — 7,000+ notes, 23,000 orphaned attachments, no coherent system.
