He translated organizational terminology into personal language, expanded discovery with personality assessments, added 360-degree validation, and built a Personal Coach that triages priorities, protects focus, and synthesizes across life domains. BradyOS helped him rebuild a business he'd shut down years ago.
Results: 14 years of scattered knowledge organized in 20 minutes. 23,000 orphans reduced to 53. The system independently identified his priority projects and told him to stop dispersing energy. In his words: "Every time I engage with it now, it very quickly gets me to where I need to go."
Same architecture — small business AI Chief of Staff and a personal system that rebuilt a career. The classification problem is universal. Starling Workspace provides the universal structure.
Deployment is conversational. No code, no setup. Guided installers extract knowledge through dialogue, producing permanent, semantically addressed memory nodes. One hour produces six foundational memories. At 23, intelligence mode activates — cross-domain synthesis without explicit instruction.

name: Panoplai
description:
Panoplai builds representative, predictive digital twins and synthetic datasets grounded in methodological rigor—not just plausible AI personas. We ingest raw quantitative data (surveys, transactional) on a columns-and-rows basis to achieve true representativeness and predictive power, then layer in unstructured data—social, transcripts—for nuanced, linguistically and emotionally accurate outputs.
What makes Panoplai different is a fundamental shift from insight-as-reporting to insight-as-decision-support. Instead of static personas or one-off studies, teams work with living audience models that can be queried, compared, and pressure-tested in real time. AI agents continuously validate and enhance output quality while guiding users through the experience.
Each digital twin gets smarter as new data is introduced and models are calibrated. It's the fusion of real, synthetic, and behavioral data in a single continuously learning system—something no competitor offers end-to-end.
how_it_works:
Panoplai centralizes audience and market data—qualitative research, surveys, behavioral signals, and performance inputs—using AI to construct dynamic audience models via digital twins that simulate response to ideas, messages, and strategies. Precisely-targeted digital twins can be spun up by users in real time.
Clients operate on a "centralized governance, decentralized usage" model: insights professionals build and validate outputs, while teams across marketing, product, and strategy integrate them into everyday decision-making.
This enables earlier validation rather than retrospective analysis. Teams explore "what-if" scenarios, compare segment-level reactions, and test assumptions without waiting for a new research cycle.
Delivered as a software platform for insights, brand, and strategy leaders, Panoplai is faster and continuously available—enabling use cases like pre-launch decision testing that episodic research alone cannot achieve.
business_need:
Traditional research is rigorous and representative but episodic, backward-looking, and arrives after decisions are made. Emerging AI approaches are fast but outputs are generic, hard to validate, and offer little value for innovation. Insights teams are stuck in the middle—between fidelity to proven methods and relentless pressure toward AI adoption.
Panoplai addresses this by enabling validated, high-quality insights to function as an always-available input to decisions—integrated into everyday decision-making across marketing, product, and innovation teams.
One example: rapidly ingesting years of tracker data on a columns-and-rows basis, combining it with transactional and unstructured opinion data, chatting with it in real time to surface quantitative trends and qualitative narratives, spinning up digital twins of targeted segments on demand, and deploying them across the organization—all backed by the full rigor of the underlying data.
business_potential:
Panoplai demonstrates strong potential across all four criteria:
Originality: Introduces a new operating model for insights—continuous, testable, and decision-first—rather than incremental improvements to traditional research.
Market Potential: The global market research industry represents $200B+ in annual spend, with 70% flowing through episodic methods. Panoplai also captures "shadow" insights spend, where leaders seek faster solutions outside traditional functions.
Scalability: Clients begin with an accelerated pilot for ~20 core users, building small-scale Client Data Universes, then expand enterprise-wide without proportional increases in cost or complexity.
Ease of Implementation: A single user can ingest raw quant or qual data in minutes, chat with their data, generate targeted digital twins, and launch new research waves—with or without consulting support.
case_study:
The following is based on a real use case from a major client organization:
A global confectionery company needed to innovate its chewing gum products to stand out in a crowded market. Young teens are the primary buyers but notoriously difficult to survey, especially given EU regulations. Timelines and budgets were constrained, and the team had no patience for unsuccessful launches.
Traditionally, they would commission focus groups and surveys, wait weeks for results, and repeat across multiple research waves—still facing internal debate about which concepts to lead with.
Using Panoplai, the team extracted maximum value from existing research, transaction, customer feedback, and performance marketing data. The platform rapidly ingested these sources and enabled new surveys to both human and synthetic audiences, bringing representative digital twins of UK and EU teen gum-chewing segments to life.
A key insight emerged: teens chew gum to improve focus during stressful moments like exams. Panoplai's digital twins—built from quant opinion, behavioral, and qual data—allowed the team to stress-test this finding for representativeness, then rapidly explore innovative solutions. Validation testing showed 91–97% consistency between digital twin responses and real human respondents.
The team tested multiple innovation concepts, compared predicted responses across segments, and identified winners—before committing to product development or marketing spend.
Projected outcomes across client organizations: 20–30% time savings across the innovation lifecycle, 40%+ reduction in research costs, and a higher concept hit rate. Rather than replacing human research, Panoplai extends its value and applies it more strategically.

name: Peekator - survey platform powered by AI workflows
description:
Peekator was created to solve a long standing problem in the survey industry. Researchers have traditionally been forced to choose between modern, visually appealing surveys with limited methodological depth or powerful research tools that feel outdated and deliver poor respondent experiences. This trade off affects data quality, engagement and speed.

The rise of AI has not resolved this tension. Instead, many AI driven research tools have made it worse by forcing teams to adopt new methodologies, connect additional platforms or add yet another tool into an already fragmented workflow. Rather than simplifying research, AI often increases complexity and pushes proven approaches aside.

Peekator takes a different approach. It brings proven research methodologies into the AI era without forcing researchers to start from scratch. By prioritising continuity over replacement, Peekator improves insight generation, analysis and interpretation while reducing fragmentation across the process.
how_it_works:
Peekator is a self serve research platform for professional research teams and insight driven brands. It enables studies using established methodologies while delivering a modern, mobile optimised survey experience.

Brands engage consumers through surveys that combine structured questions with open feedback. AI capabilities such as conversational probing and voice responses are embedded directly into the workflow, improving understanding without changing how studies are designed or analysed.

Peekator improves insight generation by increasing response quality and reducing friction across collection and analysis. Automated coding and interactive dashboards help teams move from data to insight faster.

Peekator is delivered as a single integrated platform rather than a service or add on tool, allowing teams to replace multiple research tools with one solution and reduce complexity.
business_need:
Peekator addresses a growing unmet need in the insights industry: reducing fragmentation without sacrificing research quality. Research teams increasingly rely on multiple disconnected tools for survey design, data collection, analysis, coding and reporting. While each tool may solve a specific task, this complexity reduces efficiency and negatively impacts insight quality.

As workflows become fragmented, researchers spend more time managing tools and moving data between platforms, and less time interpreting results and developing meaningful insights. The rise of AI tools has often intensified this problem by adding additional platforms rather than simplifying the process.

Peekator addresses this gap by unifying proven research methodologies, AI enhanced data collection and insight delivery within a single platform. By reducing tool overload, Peekator enables researchers to focus more on analysis, interpretation and strategic thinking, improving both the speed and quality of insights
business_potential:
Peekator’s originality lies in its philosophy rather than a single feature. While many research tools prioritise either modern user experience or advanced methodologies, Peekator combines both, bringing proven research methods into the AI era without forcing teams to change how they work.

The market potential is strong, as insight teams globally face pressure to deliver faster, higher quality insights while managing fragmented tool stacks. Peekator addresses this need across brands, agencies and research teams.

Peekator is highly scalable as a self serve, cloud based platform that can be deployed across markets and industries without services or custom implementation. New capabilities can be rolled out centrally.

Ease of implementation comes from familiarity. By supporting established methodologies and workflows, teams can adopt Peekator quickly without extensive training or process change.
case_study:
A strong example of Peekator in action is a global employee experience research study conducted by Havas People in Q4 2024. The project used Peekator’s platform to deliver a large scale B2B study across five countries, combining quantitative and qualitative research.

Over 5000 employees participated in the quantitative survey, supported by conversational AI probing embedded directly into the questionnaire. This enabled respondents to expand on their answers in a natural way, generating richer context without requiring separate qualitative tools. In parallel, 29 in depth interviews were conducted to deepen understanding of key themes.

Peekator was deployed as a single end to end platform. AI automatically coded over 38000 open ended responses and generated 104 customised PowerPoint reports for different markets and stakeholders. This significantly reduced manual analysis and reporting effort.

The study uncovered critical gaps between employee expectations and workplace realities, while accelerating insight generation and delivery. This case demonstrates how Peekator enhances proven methodologies with AI at global scale and delivers deeper insights without increasing complexity.

name: PerfectSlide
description:
Market researchers and insights professionals spend a significant amount of time creating cross-tab tables and PowerPoint slides. As a former management consultant and market researcher, I’ve been personally frustrated by how ridiculous it is to spend that much time creating tables and slides instead of extracting and interpreting insights.

Therefore, we decided to build PerfectSlide. It's an all-in-one AI survey analysis and reporting platform that can turn your survey data into client-ready PowerPoint reports in minutes or hours instead of days or weeks. It produces PowerPoint slides in your own format.
how_it_works:
Customers upload some of their real PowerPoint slides. PerfectSlide uses AI to learn the formatting and styles including hundreds of PowerPoint settings and layout rules.

Then customers can use our cross-tab tool to easily create cross-tab tables and, with one click, turn those tables into slides in their own format. Basically, there’s no real difference between a table and a slide. Once you have a table, you essentially have a slide.

Unlike traditional survey reporting tools, which either produce generic slides or require a rigid setup to link slides to Excel, PerfectSlide uses powerful AI to learn from your real slides and generate new slides in your exact format.
business_need:
We’ve filled a need for a solution that can quickly turn almost any type of raw survey data into truly client-ready PowerPoint slides, not generic slides, and not limited to a specific survey template or study type.

Current solutions either produce very generic slides that require nearly as much time to reformat as building them manually, or rely on traditional methods that link PowerPoint to Excel which is time-consuming to set up, rigid, easy to break, and typically only suitable for certain studies such as brand tracking.
business_potential:
Originality: We haven’t seen any AI solution that can truly generate client-ready survey slides. Traditional platforms either produce generic slides or require rigid, manual setup that only works for certain studies. General AI slide tools are almost not usable at all due to accuracy issues when handling quantitative data.

Market potential: PerfectSlide aims to become your AI survey analyst, handling end-to-end survey analysis and reporting for anyone who runs surveys, across industries and use cases.

Scalability: As an AI SaaS product, PerfectSlide can scale quickly. Incremental cost is mainly AI processing cost.

Ease of implementation: Very easy to set up. Customers simply need to upload a set of their existing slides and PerfectSlide will automatically learn their style and formatting.
case_study:
Step 1: We provide customers with a quick orientation session.
Step 2: Customers upload their PowerPoint template (including a set of their existing slides). PerfectSlide uses AI to learn their formatting and style.
Step 3: Customers upload their survey data and use our robust cross-tab tool to quickly build tables.
Step 4: Customers select one or multiple tables, choose their preferred chart/table types, and PerfectSlide automatically generates PowerPoint slides in their exact format.

For this application, we didn’t have time to write a full case study or obtain permission from pilot customers to share detailed results. If we’re lucky to be selected, we’d be happy to provide one or more customer case studies.

name: Persona - Predictive Answers
description:
Predictive Answers is an intelligence layer that turns research into a living, predictive system.

Brands connect qualitative research (AI-moderated or human-led in-depth interviews, focus groups), survey datasets, and other first-party research assets. We ground this research foundation in large-scale external data signals to build a dynamic insight model.

Instead of only reporting what participants said, Predictive Answers simulates how defined audience segments are likely to respond to new concepts, messaging, features, or strategic decisions before running new research.

Every prediction is traceable to underlying qualitative themes, quantitative signals, and source data. The result is forward-looking insight that is both simulated and grounded, combining innovation with methodological integrity.
data_modalities: Structured Survey; Text; No New Data
how_it_works:
Predictive Answers allows brands and research teams to ask any strategic or tactical question and receive a simulated, evidence-based answer grounded in their existing research assets.

Organizations connect qualitative transcripts, survey data, and other first-party research. Our system synthesizes these inputs with large-scale external signals to model how defined audience segments are likely to think, feel, or behave in response to new ideas.

Each response includes citations to underlying themes, a confidence score, and recommendations for further study where appropriate.

Not every question requires commissioning a new study. Predictive Answers enables instant, predictive insight without replacing research rigor. It goes beyond search or chat with your data by generating forward-looking answers, helping brands engage consumers with greater speed, precision, and strategic confidence.

Target users include enterprise brands and research agencies supporting them.
business_need:
Organizations are sitting on vast amounts of qualitative and quantitative research that remain underutilized. Insights are scattered across transcripts, surveys, trackers, and past studies, making it difficult to extract their full strategic value.

At the same time, companies are under pressure to make more insight-backed decisions, but traditional research cycles are time-consuming and expensive. As a result, many decisions are made with incomplete evidence.

Predictive Answers transforms existing research into a predictive intelligence system. By generating grounded, forward-looking answers in minutes, it saves significant time and cost. This speed enables teams to make more research-informed decisions across product, marketing, and strategy without waiting for new fieldwork.
business_potential:
Predictive Answers is original in that it moves beyond research automation or data search. It introduces a predictive intelligence layer grounded in a company’s own qualitative and quantitative research, augmented by large-scale external data. Rather than replacing research, it extends its value.

The market potential is significant. Enterprise brands and research agencies already invest heavily in primary research but lack tools to fully activate it. Predictive Answers increases the ROI of existing research budgets while accelerating decision cycles.

The platform is inherently scalable. Once research assets are connected, predictive modeling can be applied across teams, markets, and use cases without proportional cost increases.

Implementation is straightforward. Organizations upload or connect existing research assets and can begin generating predictive insight immediately, without changing their existing research workflows.
case_study:
A large dating platform uses Predictive Answers to better understand how different audience segments approach dating.

The company connects data from its internal research team into Persona. Product and growth teams can ask strategic questions such as: “How does Gen Z think about authenticity in profiles?” “How do recently divorced users approach first conversations?” or “What motivates long-term subscribers versus casual users?”

Predictive Answers generates simulated, evidence-based responses with citations to underlying themes, a confidence score, and recommended follow-up research where appropriate. Teams can also interact directly with specific segments, where AI simulates the perspective of that audience based on real research data.

All data remains private, secure, and accessible only to the brand, enabling instant predictive insight without commissioning a new study for every question.

name: Qriousity
description: Qriousity is a self-serve behavioral intelligence platform that lets brands explore what consumers actually do, not what they say or think they do. We passively collect zero-party behavioral data across iOS, Android, and desktop devices. What makes Qriousity unique is that every data point is observed and deterministic. No modeling, no inference, no recall. We build profiling, signals, data modules, and insights that are easy to understand, use, and act on. Our dynamic profiling ensures that insights are always fresh and grounded in real behavior. In our Audience Intelligence module, users can build audiences based on observed behavior, track brand and competitive engagement, and profile any audience with hundreds of behavioral variables. The result is insight generation that moves from periodic and recall-dependent to continuous, ground-truth behavioral intelligence that is AI-ready from day one.
how_it_works: Qriousity sits on top of Qrious Insight's proprietary behavioral data infrastructure. Our SDK integrates into partner apps and browser extensions through a privacy-first, fully compliant opt-in process where consumers are fairly compensated for participation. The SDK passively captures time-stamped digital activity across app usage, web behavior, shopping, location, ad exposure, and media consumption. Raw data flows daily into our infrastructure and is structured into intelligence modules that update automatically as behaviors change. Brands and research teams log into Qriousity to explore this data through an intuitive, self-serve interface. They can define audiences by observed behavior, compare segments against benchmarks, and access always-on behavioral intelligence across profiling, competitive analysis, and category trends. Our target audience is enterprise insights teams, ad agencies, and research firms.
business_need: The insights industry relies on asking people to remember and honestly report their behavior. Survey recall is increasingly unreliable, fraud is at an all-time high and jeopardizing research credibility, and respondent fatigue continues to grow. The gap between stated intent and actual behavior undermines billions in business decisions annually. Existing platforms, from legacy trackers to modern AI tools, still depend on this same flawed input layer. Qriousity addresses this by grounding every data point in observed behavior, not self-report. It answers the same questions brands ask: Who is shopping my category? Where are they going? What ads are they seeing? But with behavioral ground truth instead of recalled approximations. As AI transforms market research, the need for clean, deterministic training data is urgent. Qriousity provides exactly that: structured behavioral intelligence ready for both human analysis and machine learning.
business_potential: Behavioral data collection is positioned to become the next evolution in market research because it solves the industry's most pressing challenges at once. Fraud detection becomes longitudinal, based on continuous behavioral validation rather than one-time quality checks. Profiling becomes dynamic, updating as behaviors change without ever asking a respondent a question. Surveys become shorter and deeper when appended with observed behavioral data. Qriousity is the only self-serve platform built entirely on this approach. It doesn't layer behavioral signals onto survey results; it replaces the dependency altogether. The global market research industry exceeds $80B annually and is actively seeking these solutions.
case_study:
A major retailer wanted to understand how sports equipment shoppers navigate the digital path to purchase and where the biggest opportunities were to convert browsers into buyers. Traditional survey-based research could capture stated preferences but missed the actual shopping behavior happening inside the retailer's digital ecosystem. The gap between what shoppers said they did and what they actually did was making it difficult to optimize the digital shopping experience.
Using Qriousity, we tracked 5,700+ real shopping journeys from search to purchase, all passively observed through behavioral data. No surveys, no recall, no friction. The analysis captured the full journey: how shoppers entered the category, what search terms they used, how they compared products across brands, which product pages they engaged with, where they dropped off, and what behavioral triggers separated casual browsers from committed buyers.
Key findings included the identification of specific friction points in the purchase funnel that survey data had never surfaced. The behavioral data revealed that a significant portion of high-intent shoppers were abandoning at a predictable stage in the journey, something that stated-preference research had completely missed. The data also uncovered which competitor brands were winning consideration during the comparison phase and what content was influencing the final purchase decision.
The outcome: the retailer refined their product assortment and merchandising strategy based on observed navigation behavior, optimized their digital shelf placement using real engagement data, and built a competitive win strategy grounded in what shoppers actually did. The behavioral approach replaced a quarterly survey process with continuous, always-current intelligence that could be acted on in real time.

name: Questra.ai
description:
Questra is your new, AI-powered survey programmer! Today, your surveys take days (or weeks) to go from questionnaire to live survey – a process that often costs thousands of dollars per survey. 

With Questra, those same surveys will be programmed in minutes at a fraction of the price. Just upload your questionnaire, wait a couple minutes, and export to your existing survey platform so you can keep doing what you do best: research!
how_it_works: Just upload your questionnaire to our platform and watch as our AI programs your survey in realtime. When programming is done, often in as little as 3-4 minutes, you can export your survey to your existing platform (Decipher, ConfirmIt, Qualtrics, and more) without changing anything else about your research operations. No more days of waiting, over spending, or capacity issues - just more research.
business_need: Speed and time – easy. Researchers are under the gun more than ever to deliver insights faster and cheaper. Automating survey programming not only lets them satisfy those pressures, but it does so without compromising the actual research at all.
business_potential:
- 1000x faster survey programming - shaves days of time off EVERY study
- 10x cheaper than human labor to increase research margins or pass through savings to clients
- Unlimited capacity - no more waiting for programmer availability or paying surge charges
case_study:
960 surveys/year. 8.5 hours each. $600K–$1.2M in programming costs. 

That was the reality for a mid-sized research agency. The result? High costs, long turnarounds, and bandwidth so tight they sometimes had to turn down work.

Then they switched to Questra:

- Cost per survey: $708,100+ hours saved (equivalent of 4+ FTEs worth)
- Up to $1.15M annual savings (using onshore employees)
- Faster turnaround + no more bottlenecks

Questra didn’t replace their team—it freed them to focus on design, analytics, reporting and interacting with clients.

--------

Our model is simple: we don’t get paid unless the programming works. That’s how strongly we believe in the platform, and we’re willing to bet our future on outcomes, not promises.

To make it easy to validate it's efficacy via testing, every new user gets two free credits. New users can test us side-by-side with their existing processes, risk-free.

name: Scalafai Studio
description: Scalafai, Studio, is the only agentic AI-first solution, built for researchers by researchers, that can automate the entire end to end research process: from design, collection, analysis, and reporting with 20+ years of industry expertise built in.
how_it_works:
Researchers describe their objectives, and Studio writes methodology-aware survey drafts, instantly programs them, makes it easy to buy sample, and automatically applies three-stage data quality checks (pre-survey fraud screening, in-survey attention monitoring, post-survey validation). Crosstab, tables, analysis, presentations, and infographic deliverables are generated automatically.

Studio eliminates the manual bottlenecks between research stages, enabling faster iteration and deeper analysis. Research agencies, enterprise insights teams, and professional services firms can run more studies, test more hypotheses, and deliver insights in days instead of weeks.

Studio is a modular platform, not another point solution. Teams can adopt individual components (ex. presentation generator) or the full workflow. It integrates with existing tools (Decipher, Confirmit, Qualtrics), is SOC 2 compliant, and is built by research veterans with 20+ years of industry experience.
business_need:
The restech landscape has become the problem it was supposed to solve. With 300+ platforms fragmenting the research workflow, teams spend more time managing tools than generating insights. Each platform handles one piece: survey design here, data processing there, reporting somewhere else, creating a labyrinth of logins, exports, and manual handoffs that drains budgets and delays decisions. 

Scalafai Studio eliminates this fragmentation. Our AI-native platform unifies the entire research workflow in one powerful environment. Teams replace multiple tools with one, cutting costs while accelerating time-to-insight from weeks to days. Simplicity drives better research.
business_potential: We built the first AI-native quant platform from scratch—no legacy tech debt holding us back. While incumbents like Qualtrics and Decipher struggle to bolt on AI, and newcomers can't handle professional-grade complexity, Studio fills that gap. It's modular, SOC 2 compliant, and scales with your team. No boring training course required. Just speak English in AI chat to interact with the platform, or push the buttons in an easy to user interface. As one user put it: "I can tell whoever built this has done this before."
case_study:
A mid-size research agency fields a 2,000-respondent brand tracker across five markets. Traditionally, this means weeks of manual survey programming, constant sample monitoring, and analysts spending days cleaning data before building a PowerPoint deck.

With Studio, the project lead describes the study in plain English. The AI builds the questionnaire, applies methodology guardrails, and programs it instantly. During fielding, Studio's three-stage data cleaning works in real-time to find fraudulent and inattentive responses before they contaminate the dataset. A "complete" that fills your quota, is actual "good complete". Once quotas are filled, automated analysis generates deliverables that reduce delivery time by at least 50%.

We're already piloting Studio with research teams. Since Studio imports survey data from any platform, (ex. Decipher, Confirmit, Qualtrics) pilot users can test data cleaning and instant deliverable creation against their own projects. 

A pilot user recently commented  they wished they'd had Studio's real-time sample cleaning on a previous project. Poor-quality respondents forced a complete re-field that put their client relationship at risk. Studio would have caught those issues mid-field, saving the project and the relationship damage.

name: Score Metrics Alternative to Net Promoter Score
description: My Sister in Law has a small destination management company in Dallas that she's been running for 30 years.  She recently started the process of selling it.  The consultant they hired strongly suggested that she needed to establish a net promoter score program to show the customer sentiment towards her company.  Implementing such a program was easy, but its only valuable to the consultant.  It tells my sister in law nothing about how they are doing.  I saw that we could develop a plug and play approach to NPS using my Sort & Score technique to add depth of competitive context to NPS while making it easy for small business customers to have an elevated marketing research tool that provides information.
how_it_works: through our website, our customer can send us their customer list, set a group of competitors and tell us how many times to reach out to them.  We'll run the study through SMS or Email, collect the data and make it available to them on our dashboard.  The programming is GDPR level privacy and security.  It's the kind of service that large corporate entities get, but available to everyone.  And it makes our clients better because they can see how their customers view them relative to their competitors.  It's quick, easy, inexpensive and good research design.  It's better, faster and cheaper than what enterprise brands are using to measure themselves today.
business_need: The most important.  How do my customers view me, my product, my service and my brand relative to those brands I see as my competition.  And how much better or worse am I.  Sort and score not only delivers a competitive context, but the design produces a relative value similar to MaxDiff.
business_potential: while it is originally conceived for small business, all of the components are there so that enterprise businesses can take advantage of the design and the tool as well.  The opportunity is as large as the entire Net Promoter Score business and so much more, because it is so easy to use, the market is now open to any business that wants to know where they stand with their customers.  This is NPS 2.0 and it's much better.
case_study: OK.  Let's pretend.  My local coffee shop, Juniper Goods in Longmont Colorado, has three coffee shops they see as their competition within a 2 block area of their store.  Full Sail Bakery, Juliette's, and Main Street Coffee.  Juniper good's manager goes out to our website (scoremetrics.com), signs up to do a study, attaches their customer list in excel, adds in their set of competitors and asks us to reach out to their list 3 times in the next month via sms.  The programming sets up their QNR, adds in their competitive set and sends out to their list.  The manager can watch the incoming completes as they come in.  Respondents are asked two questions: 1. please sort these coffee shops in order of your likelihood to recommend them to friends; then 2. with these coffee shops in your order of likelihood to recommend, allocate 100 points to show me how much you are likely to recommend one over another.  If the only one you would recommend is the first one, then give it 100 points and the rest a zero.  If they are all equal, then share the points.  The dashboard adds up all of the scores in Q2 for each shop, divides by the number of completes and multiplies by the number of brands.  It's simple, easy to use and very easy to explain.  Even an overworked, under staffed CEO or head bottle washer will get it.

name: Survey Fluency
description: Survey Fluency is a survey analysis platform designed to remove the manual work between raw data and decision-ready insights. Analysts ask questions in plain language while the system handles survey parsing, audience definition, cross-tabs, diagnostics, and reporting. What makes it different is that it reasons about survey structure and analytical validity before producing results and links every output back to the source variables. The result is faster analysis, clearer interpretation, and client-ready Word and PowerPoint outputs without sacrificing transparency or rigor.
how_it_works: Users upload standard survey data files and interact with them through natural language. Behind the scenes, AI agents interpret the survey design, manage complex logic and samples, run analysis, and generate explanations alongside tables and charts. This allows brands to understand consumer attitudes, behaviors, and experiences more quickly while enabling analysts to run deeper analysis and learn from the process. Survey Fluency serves individual researchers, insights teams, and research organizations and is delivered as a product platform that supports ongoing, iterative analysis rather than static reporting.
business_need: Survey Fluency addresses the time-to-insight and capacity gap in the global insights industry. Practitioners report that chart generation and formatting account for roughly 75% of the workload in a typical survey project, which means analysts earning around $100,000 per year spend most of their time on manual data entry, cleaning, weighting, and proofing rather than interpretation and strategy. Real-world datasets often include tens or hundreds of thousands of variables, complex logic, stacked questions, and multiple sample types such as core and boost samples. This slows insight delivery by weeks, reduces the number of projects a team can take on by 30–40%, and results in roughly $200,000 in lost revenue per year from delayed decisions, missed opportunities, and unused analyst capacity.
business_potential: Survey Fluency fills a gap between traditional survey platforms and generic analytics or AI tools. Survey platforms like Qualtrics and SurveyMonkey stop at data collection and leave analysis manual, while tools such as SPSS, R, Python, and BI dashboards require significant expertise and time to translate data into decisions. Survey Fluency automates end-to-end survey analysis so analysts spend less time in clunky tools and more time interpreting results. The product is designed for market research firms, political pollsters, and in-house insights teams and targets a large and growing market, starting with a $250M wedge and expanding into a $45B survey and insights software segment, with long-term relevance across the broader $150B global insights market. The chat interface and AI coding capability allow even junior analysts to use the platform easily, produce robust results, and deliver client-ready reports in hours rather than weeks.
case_study:
Our design partner is KL Communications (KLC), a market research firm specializing in community-based research. Starting in October 2025, KLC's President Dan Womack walked us through their end-to-end workflow and identified their core pain point: reporting. After a survey closes, KLC sends data to an offshore tabulation partner, waits for cross-tabs, then analysts manually build PowerPoint decks, format charts, and write executive summaries—a week-long cycle. By then, clients have often already made their decisions and moved on.
Over three months of weekly collaboration, KLC's team directly shaped the product. They corrected our industry terminology, pushed for editable PowerPoint charts instead of static images, advocated for Excel upload support alongside SPSS, and insisted on citation-level transparency so every AI-generated insight traces back to its source survey question. Their senior research director Tracy noted that until we demonstrated advanced capabilities like clustering and analytical plans, she assumed the platform only did basic summaries—leading us to build guided prompts so users discover the full depth organically. Berkeley, their operations lead, confirmed the platform felt intuitive even as a non-researcher, validating our UX approach.
KLC is now running a live pilot on an actual client project—testing the Analysis Engine side-by-side with their traditional workflow under real delivery pressure. Dan defined success: if the platform produces client-quality deliverables with minimal manual finishing, they'll adopt it across their quantitative practice. He also evaluated a competing tool and found it lacking: "It's a pragmatic tool but not a very smart tool." KLC's goal is to turn week-long reporting into same-day turnaround, freeing analysts to focus on strategic interpretation rather than slide assembly.

name: Swytchback
description: We’ve modernized traditional research by making best-in-class methodologies instant, visual, and accessible. Our mobile-first design delivers the same proven methods used by the largest research firms in an engaging, intuitive experience – at a fraction of the cost. On our platform, MaxDiff is fun and visual to increase respondent engagement, and automated TURF analysis makes deep insights available instantly. The result is faster insight generation, clearer interpretation, and confident decisions driven by real demand from the right consumers, exactly when brands need to act. We are also well suited to be deployed in field, including for in home user testing, based on our mobile-optimized design and ability to detect when test product is being sampled on a real time basis.
how_it_works:
We partner with panel providers to reach brands’ exact target consumers, using a visual interface that engages shoppers more effectively and delivers quick, precise insights. Brands and retailers use our platform to test package designs, flavors, scents, messaging, product concepts, on-package claims, proof of demand, and more.

Our target audience includes scaling brands that need to prove demand to investors and retailers, insights teams seeking faster, repeatable, data-driven decisions, retailers optimizing assortment, and site-based businesses tracking real-time consumer reactions.

Unlike traditional text-heavy surveys or AI-generated panels, our platform leverages images – processed by the brain 60,000 times faster than text – to generate deeper, more accurate insights. By making surveys fun for consumers and effortless for brands, we provide insights that are faster, more engaging, and more actionable than conventional research methods.
business_need: Brands and retailers lack access to deep, real-time insights from real consumers without incurring tens of thousands of dollars in traditional research costs. Existing solutions are often slow, expensive, or rely on generic (or AI) panels that don’t capture the nuances of target audiences. Our platform addresses this gap by delivering precise, decision-ready insights quickly, affordably, and directly from the consumers brands actually care about – enabling smarter, faster decisions that were previously unattainable through conventional research methods.  In addition, we support a better form of in-site testing based on our mobile design and ability to detect real time sampling through our integrated sensor app.
business_potential: We are the first and only visually-focused research platform that combines engaging, mobile-first surveys with instant TURF analysis, enabling brands to maximize market reach and make confident, data-driven decisions at a fraction of the cost of traditional research. Our platform is highly scalable, supporting brands of all sizes – from emerging startups validating demand to established enterprises optimizing product assortments. We integrate seamlessly with existing research systems and workflows, ensuring ease of implementation without disrupting current processes. By making advanced research methodologies accessible, faster, and more actionable, we unlock market potential previously unattainable through conventional research solutions. And we can execute in home and in-venue testing on a much more effective basis that in-person intercepts and traditional focus groups.
case_study:
A functional food brand developing a new product uses our platform to validate demand before pitching retailers. The team tests multiple package designs, flavors, and on-pack claims (e.g., benefits, texture, ingredient callouts) with verified consumers who regularly purchase hydration and functional beverage products at similar stores. Visual stimuli are uploaded to the platform to create their survey and deployed through partnered panel providers to reach the brand’s exact target shopper.

Consumers interact with the concepts through a visual-first interface, allowing the brand to see which combination of packaging, flavor expectations, and claims drives attention, preference, and purchase intent. Results are delivered instantly, enabling the team to refine the winning concept before production and retail outreach.

The platform is deployed as a lightweight, self-serve, or managed solution that integrates into existing research workflows. This approach has been used by early adopter brands to generate proof of demand and de-risk launch decisions ahead of investor meetings and retailer conversations.

name: System1
description:
AI has transformed how quickly ideas can be generated, but not how confidently they can be evaluated. Teams can now produce thousands of concepts in minutes, yet still struggle to identify which will succeed.

System1’s Test Your Innovation, combined with BionicX AI, closes this gap. It integrates generative AI with predictive, emotion-based testing to create a closed-loop innovation system. Ideas are not just generated at scale, but evaluated based on how real people are likely to feel and respond.

This approach is differentiated by its focus on emotional response as a leading indicator of commercial success. While most AI tools optimize for speed, this solution ensures ideas are grounded in human truth.

The result is a shift from speculative innovation to evidence-based decision making, enabling teams to prioritize ideas with confidence before investing in market.
how_it_works:
This solution connects idea generation and validation into a single workflow.

BionicX AI rapidly generates product, campaign, or brand ideas based on defined inputs. These ideas are then tested using System1’s Test Your Innovation platform, which predicts real-world performance by measuring emotional response.

Using validated behavioral science, the platform assesses how strongly ideas are likely to engage, resonate, and drive action. Outputs clearly identify which ideas to progress, refine, or reject.

This creates a continuous loop: generate, test, optimize, and act. Results are delivered in as little as 48 hours, enabling rapid iteration at scale.

The platform is designed for brand, insights, and innovation teams, particularly in consumer-facing industries. It replaces slow, traditional research with a scalable, platform-based approach that validates AI-generated ideas quickly and effectively.
business_need:
Generative AI has fundamentally changed innovation, but it has also broken it.

Teams can now generate hundreds of ideas in minutes, yet they are left with a new problem: which ones are actually worth investing in. Volume has increased, but confidence has not.

Traditional research was not designed for this reality. It is too slow, too expensive, and too limited to keep pace with AI-driven ideation. As a result, businesses are making bigger decisions with less certainty, relying on instinct, internal bias, or incomplete data.

This is not just inefficient. It is risky.

This solution addresses a critical gap by bringing predictive, human truth into AI-led innovation. By measuring emotional response, it identifies which ideas are most likely to resonate and drive real-world success before they reach market.

In a world where anything can be created, knowing what will work is the new competitive advantage.
business_potential:
This solution delivers strong originality, scalability, and market relevance.

Originality: It combines generative AI with predictive, emotion-based testing, shifting innovation from idea volume to validated effectiveness.

Market Potential: As AI accelerates idea generation, organizations need tools to evaluate those ideas with confidence. This creates demand for solutions that combine speed with reliable insight.

Scalability: The platform can be applied across product innovation, creative development, and brand strategy, and scaled across markets and categories.

Ease of Implementation: It integrates into existing workflows and delivers results quickly, enabling immediate adoption without complex setup.

By replacing guesswork with predictive confidence, this solution allows organizations to move faster while increasing the likelihood of success.
case_study:
Imagine a global food brand looking to unlock its next wave of snacking innovation. Traditionally, this would involve months of research, multiple agencies, and significant investment, yet still leave uncertainty about which ideas will truly resonate with consumers.

The combined System1 and BionicX AI solution fundamentally changes this process.

First, BionicX AI analyzes large-scale, real-world consumer data, such as online reviews and behavioral signals, to uncover unmet needs, emotional drivers, and emerging trends. From this, it generates innovation territories and concept ideas grounded in authentic consumer language rather than assumption.

These ideas are then rapidly validated using System1’s Test Your Innovation platform, which predicts emotional response and commercial potential. This enables teams to quickly understand which ideas will connect, which need refinement, and which should be deprioritized.

The result is not just insight, but clear, actionable direction. Teams can confidently prioritize concepts, refine positioning, and build stronger pipelines based on predicted real-world impact.

In a recent case study with a global food manufacturer, this approach replaced a high six-figure traditional research program and delivered results in under three weeks. AI-generated concepts were tested and iterated, with all optimized ideas outperforming their original versions on key effectiveness metrics.

The solution is deployed as a scalable workflow that integrates seamlessly into existing innovation processes, enabling teams to generate, test, and improve ideas continuously.

By combining generative AI with predictive validation, organizations can innovate faster, reduce risk, and bring more effective ideas to market with confidence.

name: Talk&Act
description:
PersonaTwin helps researchers and brands turn disconnected historical studies into reusable, data backed persona twins that can react to new stimuli. Instead of treating personas as static narratives, PersonaTwin uses probabilistic persona inference to identify respondents from past research who best represent a desired persona definition, even when the relevant signals come from different studies and question wordings.
A key differentiator is our canonical mapping layer. We map each study’s questions into a shared ontology of study types, sections, intents, and indicators, so multiple surveys can be combined without losing meaning. This unlocks consistent persona creation, measurable sample coverage, and transparent confidence scoring.
PersonaTwin then lets teams test concepts, claims, messaging, and other stimuli with these inferred persona twins to get directional reactions in historical respondent behavior. Teams can later validate outputs by running a pointed followup survey.
data_modalities: Structured Survey; Text; No New Data
how_it_works:
Users create a project and upload survey data and internal documents. PersonaTwin parses the inputs and maps variables into a shared research ontology (study type, section, intent, indicator). This produces a unified respondent level dataset across studies, even when wording, scales, and variable names differ.
To build a persona, users describe the audience they want. PersonaTwin applies probabilistic persona inference that combines explicit rules (when fields are available) with semantic evidence (when studies express the same idea differently). It reports matched sample size, coverage across required constructs, key differentiators, and confidence.
Users then test stimuli such as concepts, claims, taglines, and messaging in a chat based workflow. Reactions are generated by referencing the historically similar respondent set behind each persona twin, and outputs remain traceable to the mapped constructs and supporting evidence. Also, teams can validate by surveying real respondents.
business_need:
Most insights teams have years of research, but it is trapped in disconnected studies, inconsistent question wording, and one-off recodes. Reusing learnings across studies is slow and often unreliable, which leads to repeated fieldwork and missed opportunities to leverage what is already known.
Persona work and stimulus evaluation face the same issue. Personas are often narrative led, hard to validate, and not clearly tied back to real respondent behavior. PersonaTwin addresses this gap by standardizing studies into a shared ontology and using probabilistic inference to create persona twins grounded in historically similar respondents. This enables fast, consistent, data backed stimulus reactions, with a clear path to validation through small follow up studies.
business_potential:
PersonaTwin targets a large and growing need in the global insights ecosystem: faster insight generation with higher consistency and better reuse of existing research investments. The concept is original in how it combines a canonical mapping layer with probabilistic persona inference, producing personas with measurable coverage, uncertainty, and traceability, rather than static descriptions.
The platform is scalable because the ontology based mapping generalizes across categories and study types, and it supports brand scoped, multi-tenant workspaces for both agencies and individual brands. Implementation is straightforward because teams can start from common survey exports and existing internal documents, then expand project by project without requiring a large research ops or data science team.
case_study:
Case Study: Turning Historical Research into Probabilistic Persona Twins
A global consumer electronics brand had over 10 years of research across trackers, U&A studies, and concept tests. Despite this investment, insights remained siloed due to inconsistent question wording, formats, and one-off recodes. New messaging tests required fresh fieldwork.
The brand piloted PersonaTwin to assess whether historical data could power reusable, data-backed persona twins.
Eighteen legacy datasets and internal documents were uploaded. PersonaTwin mapped variables into a shared research ontology (study type, section, intent, indicator), aligning differently worded questions into consistent constructs (e.g., environmental concern, innovation affinity). This created a unified respondent-level dataset across ~38,000 historical respondents.
The team defined an “Eco-Conscious Innovator” persona. PersonaTwin applied probabilistic persona inference, combining explicit rules and semantic evidence across studies. Instead of a binary segment, respondents received membership probabilities. Outputs included matched sample size (3,420), construct coverage, and confidence scoring.
Three new sustainability claims were then tested via the chat interface. Reactions were generated by referencing historically similar respondents and mapped constructs. The system predicted strongest resonance for “Innovation That Reduces Your Carbon Footprint,” citing alignment between sustainability commitment and innovation adoption signals.
To validate, the brand ran a targeted follow-up survey (n=250) using the same persona criteria. Claim ranking matched PersonaTwin’s prediction, with strong directional correlation.
Impact:
-Reduced exploratory fieldwork
-Cut screening time from weeks to days
-Replaced static personas with measurable, data-backed twins
Improved transparency via coverage and confidence reporting

name: Tapestry.AI
description:
Imagine, you could "scroll through" a feed, but it wasn't yours, it was a feed of a (consenting) customer. Imagine how much more rich, qualitative insight could be gained from it. 

We are in an age where media consumption and digital behaviour is very personalized and fragmented, and existing tooling does not yet allow for a a marketer to intuitively review this data in a way that sticks.

Our innovations are part social, part technological, part strategy, and part governance.

Social: increase consumer panelist trust in ecosystem by using clear UX to "show" data being used very interactively.
Technological: deploy AI agents in an agentic world scanner.
Strategy: multi-sided platform.
Governance: non-profit data collection arm requires a focus on the panelist's well being.
how_it_works:
It is a multi-sided data exchange platform, which includes apps that provide consumer value in the mental health space, traditional SaaS dashboarding views, agentic workflows for data collection and validation, and transparency user experiences to enhance trust within the panel collection ecosystem.

Functionally:
It allows marketers to see what consumers see:

Think:
Scroll through the a feed of a user.
Target Audience is market research firms, creative agencies, and eventually brand direct. It is a multi-sided platform, focused on a services model to start.

Technically: it includes a strategic mix of collection methods, ranging from screenshots, to legal scraping, to. It uses the latest privacy preserving methods (edge processing, homographic encryption, differential privacy, and synthetic data augmentation) to maintain panelist trust.
business_need:
1. No user friendly way to view and engage with item level media consumption data.
2. Disconnect between media consumption research, and creative advertising workflows, and consumer insights platforms.
3. Existence of robust audience measurement / ad measurement ecosystem, but lack of tooling for understanding consumer behaviour and identity based on non-ad, soon to be personally generated content to which we spend more and time on our phones consuming.
business_potential:
Originality of concept: Patent Pending - according to our competitive research, patent search, and experience working in the market, we believe this is unique.

Our team: a blend of scientists, consultants, and data engineers.
Market Potential: This depends on the manner to which it this new data type can proxy against existing market insights. Our estimates range from $15B to $75B TAM. 
Ease of Implementation.
case_study:
James, a strategic planner at WPP, creates an audience group for his client Coca Cola. He sees that this audience has a peculiar knack for consuming Tiktok content about younger people taking up knitting, shot in crowded downtown city condos. 75% more than the population average. Interesting. Survey shows that they consume this content for relaxation purposes.

He takes this, and shares this insight to his creative team. His team now has a qualitative insight backed by quantitative measures.

They produce an ad campaign shot within that native visual context (condo apartment, knitting, relaxing video), that achieves a much higher thruplay / engagement rate at a much lower cost.

The campaign wins creative awards for its ability to get deep into the textured qual, whilst maintaining statistical rigor.
Illustrative, piloting in June.

name: The Data Alchemy Platform
description:
The Data Alchemy Platform is a self-service analytics suite making sophisticated data science accessible to insight professionals.

In market research, the most powerful techniques (machine learning, deep learning, generative AI) require a data scientist to execute. But in 20 years' experience, the communication gap between data scientists and client-facing researchers erodes their value. The data scientist can't fully interpret the brief; the research executive can't fully articulate the output. Insight gets lost in translation.

Data Alchemy eliminates that gap. It distils the 20% of techniques that elevate 80% of research data analysis into six apps (i.e. Description, Distillation, Segmentation, Prediction, Synthetic Data, and The Data Alchemist AI advisor) wrapping ML driver analysis, neural network index creation and generative AI data augmentation in guided interfaces any research professional can operate.
how_it_works:
Users upload data into the platform: Description to explore, generate hypotheses and clean variables with AI assistance; Distillation to create composite indices via dimension reduction; Prediction to identify what drives outcomes using ML with AI-generated strategic recommendations; Segmentation to build outcome-based groupings feeding back into Prediction to understand each segment's drivers. Synthetic Data enables imputation or projection of surveys onto larger populations. The Data Alchemist AI advisor consults separately on which tool to use and when. This gives researchers direct access to the 'why' behind consumer attitudes and behaviours, driver hierarchies, motivational segments and strategic recommendations that previously required commissioning a data science team.
Target audience: Insight professionals and research teams at brands and agencies, particularly organisations without dedicated data science resource.
Go-to-market: self-service subscription platform
business_need:
The biggest unmet need in the insights industry is a lack of accessible, actionable analysis. The most powerful analytics require either hiring a data scientist (costly and often failing to deliver ROI) or purchasing analytics platforms priced beyond the reach of most insight teams.

Even when data science resource is available, the communication gap between technical specialists and client-facing researchers remains. The data scientist misinterprets the business brief. The research executive cannot fully articulate the analytical output to stakeholders. Sophisticated analysis loses its power in translation  and the insight never lands.

The Data Alchemy Platform addresses both barriers: it eliminates the dependency on data science intermediaries by wrapping advanced methods in guided interfaces that research professionals can operate directly. The person closest to the business question controls the analysis, interprets the output and delivers the insight.
business_potential:
Originality: Methodology-first, not generic statistics in a UI. Proprietary apps built from 20 years of consulting, distilling the 20% of techniques that elevate 80% of research analysis. The connected journey, where each app's output feeds the next, is unique in self-service analytics.
Market potential: Sits in the gap between basic DIY tools ($500–2K/year) and enterprise platforms ($22K–125K+/year). Insight teams without data science resource represent the largest underserved segment globally.
Scalability: Four subscription tiers from free trial to Pro (or custom builds). Modular architecture - new apps added without disrupting existing tools. No data stored, minimising infrastructure and data security (i.e. GDPR). AI advisor and automated email coaching eliminate onboarding support overhead.
Ease of implementation: Browser-based, no installation. First analytical output within 30 minutes of sign-up. No procurement friction and easily accessible with tiers starting from $75/month.
case_study:
Knowsis contributed advanced analytics to Intrum's 2025 European Consumer Payment Report, a flagship study of 20,000 consumers across 20 European countries, published November 2025.
The challenge: create a Money Management Index that measures financial health beyond income, then identify what drives it and segment consumers into actionable personas.

Using the Data Alchemy Platform's connected journey: Distillation compressed multiple debt and financial confidence variables into a single composite index score per consumer. Prediction identified the behavioural and attitudinal drivers of financial resilience. Segmentation grouped consumers into four Money Management personas, from 'Self-disciplined optimisers' to 'Debt-burdened and financially fragile', each with tailored business recommendations. The output shaped a 28-page published report informing credit strategy across Europe's largest debt management company. 

Previously, this analysis would require a dedicated data science team. With the Data Alchemy Platform, insight teams like Intrum's could run this entire analytical workflow themselves, no data scientist required.

name: The India Core
description: While India’s culture is vast and valuable, it remains fragmented and inconsistently represented in modern commerce. We are building a credibility-first cultural commerce platform that blends verified information, community validation, and high-quality curated products: transforming how Indian heritage is discovered, trusted, and purchased.
how_it_works:
1. Product Credibility

To eliminate trust gaps in cultural commerce, every product will undergo third-party lab certification wherever applicable.

This will ensure: Verified material authenticity, quality assurance, standardized benchmarks, transparent documentation

Our objective is to move Indian cultural products from “emotion-driven purchases” to evidence-backed premium offerings.

2. Information Layer

We have developed: A dedicated website that serves as a structured knowledge hub on India

name: VERA
description:
VERA is a next‑generation qualitative insight framework built to uncover the real drivers of patient and clinician decision‑making. While traditional qualitative research captures what people say, it often stops short of explaining why behaviors occur. VERA closes that gap by systematically mapping every quote to four underlying forces, Values, Preferences, Assumptions, and Constraints, revealing the logic people use when navigating diagnosis, treatment, adherence, and care.
This is the innovation: VERA turns qualitative interviews into a structured decision‑making map. Instead of relying on thematic summaries or subjective interpretation, teams gain a clear, reproducible view of how decisions are formed and where they break down. The framework brings rigor and consistency to qualitative insights, making behavioral drivers visible, comparable, and actionable across studies.
Most qualitative frameworks describe themes. VERA explains behavior.
how_it_works:
VERA is a behavioral‑insight framework that transforms qualitative interviews into a structured map of the forces that drive human decisions. Instead of relying on thematic summaries or anecdotal interpretation, VERA codes every quote into one of four underlying behavioral drivers:
Values — what matters most to the individual (identity, autonomy, normalcy)
Preferences — what they want or avoid in treatments, care, or communication
Assumptions — what they believe to be true (accurate or not)
Constraints — what limits their choices (logistics, fears, access, comorbidities)
Target audiences are insights, brand, and marketing teams in pharma, biotech, device, etc.
This is a consultative framework using AI-augmented analyses that take traditional qualitative insights and transform them into a behavioral roadmap.
business_need:
VERA captures why people behave the way they do.
This gives brands a deeper, more psychologically grounded understanding of: 
treatment decisions
adherence barriers
emotional drivers
identity disruption
belief systems
risk interpretation
lifestyle impacts
business_potential:
VERA is a breakthrough in qualitative insight generation because it:
reveals the true drivers of behavior
brings rigor and reproducibility to qual
turns interviews into strategic decision maps
scales across studies and teams
enables insights that traditional methods cannot
accelerates the path from raw data to actionable strategy

name: ZCoded
description:
Most decisions in marketing, media and brand communications are still made after money is spent. We change that.
Acutus AI’s ZCoded is a pre-market decision intelligence platform that uses AI-powered digital twins to simulate real consumer behaviour before launch. We combine first-party behavioural data, zero-party inputs, and contextual signals to build high-fidelity personas and run thousands of “what-if” scenarios across creatives, concepts and brand communication scripts.
Our differentiation lies in two areas: a decision simulation engine and a category specific proprietary data moat. Models are trained on structured behavioural patterns and continuously enriched datasets, enabling replication of how consumers decide, not just what they say.
This improves insights by accelerating generation (no fieldwork delays), enabling scenario-based analysis, and delivering clear persona-level interpretation, turning research into a predictive sandbox for decision-making.
notes: EU 2026 Rank 14, Score 34.67. Crispin Beale: ZCoded addresses a real market gap with a well-framed concept and a standout case study. The platform's core value proposition â€” test before you spend â€” is clear and commercially relevant.  | Kristi Zuhlke: the modeling or the framing of consumer decisions is what makes this different from others but what is their methodology?
how_it_works:
ZCoded works as a simulation pipeline from data to decisions. We ingest first-party, zero-party, and research data to build AI-powered digital twin personas using behavioural attributes, motivations, and cognitive decision frameworks. Brands then define scenarios testing, media, scripts and concepts across context. Our AI decision engine runs thousands of simulations, applying trade-offs and heuristics to replicate real consumer choices.
The output goes beyond scores, delivering preference shifts, behavioural distributions, and “why” behind decisions at a persona level, enabling deeper understanding of attitudes and real-world behaviour.
Our target users are brands, agencies and insights teams across CPG, D2C and SaaS.
We go to market as a SaaS platform with optional services, not a traditional research project. It is faster (hours vs weeks), privacy-safe, and enables pre-market, scenario-based insights that are difficult or impossible to capture through conventional methods.
business_need:
The global insights industry is built to explain what already happened, not what will happen. Brands still rely on surveys, small samples, and post-campaign analytics. These are slow, expensive, and increasingly unreliable in a world of fragmented attention, rising respondent fatigue, and declining data quality. At the same time, privacy regulations are restricting access to real consumer data, making large-scale testing harder.
This creates a clear unmet need: a scalable, privacy-safe way to understand and predict real consumer decisions before market exposure.
ZCoded addresses this gap by shifting insights from data collection to decision simulation. Using AI-powered digital twins built on first-party and zero-party data, brands can test scenarios at scale and understand not just what consumers say, but how they behave across contexts.
It enables faster, more reliable, and forward-looking insights, something traditional research was never designed to deliver.
business_potential:
ZCoded introduces a new category—pre-market decision intelligence—shifting insights from post-facto measurement to predictive simulation, making it highly original in a space dominated by surveys and analytics tools.
Market Potential: The global CX and insights market exceeds $20B+, with growing demand for faster, privacy-safe alternatives. Rising data costs, stricter regulations, and declining response quality are accelerating adoption of synthetic and AI-led approaches.
Scalability: Built as a SaaS platform, ZCoded scales rapidly across use cases (creative testing, concept test, PMF) and industries (CPG, D2C, SaaS). Simulation allows thousands of scenarios to be tested without incremental fieldwork costs.
Ease of Implementation: Brands can plug in existing data (surveys, CRM, briefs) and start testing within hours—no panels, no fieldwork. This reduces time-to-insight from weeks to hours, making adoption friction low and ROI immediate.
case_study:
A global luggage brand tests a new “suspension wheels + cup holder” campaign using ZCoded before launch. Instead of running a traditional pre-test with limited respondents, they simulate the ad across thousands of digital twin personas built from travel behaviour and purchase intent data.

The simulation reveals a nuanced story. While the creative scores high on memorability (81%) and quality (84%), engagement and conversion intent are only moderate.

More importantly, the platform identifies why. Viewers love the “no-spill on cobblestones” feature, but decision friction comes from missing pricing, availability, and customer reviews—highlighted repeatedly in persona-level reasoning.

ZCoded doesn’t stop at diagnosis—it prescribes action:

Move key proof moments to the first 2–5 seconds to improve scroll-stop rates
Add benefit-first messaging (“No spills. No stress.”)
Introduce trust signals (reviews, warranty cues)
Modify CTA to preview pricing and delivery details

The result is not just a report, but a pre-market optimization strategy—helping the brand refine creative, messaging, and targeting before spending on media.

Deployment is simple: brands upload creatives and inputs, define scenarios, and receive simulation outputs within hours.

This replaces weeks of research with real-time, scenario-driven insights, enabling brands to move from guesswork to confident, data-backed decisions before going live.

name: Platinum

how_it_works:
Our audience is corporate insight teams that need immediate answers from multiple data sources, from a system that understands their organisation, stakeholders and roles. They are time-poor and need the power of agentic workflows combined with the reliability of traditional methods. 

Platinum accesses multiple data sources via files and API: survey data, CRM extracts, social media feeds. Users plan a workflow with the agent, which then generates outputs: tabulations through our research-grade engine, tagging of survey and social media comments with theme and emotion, or writing Python, HTML and JavaScript to deliver interactive web applications. 

The critical differentiator: our agents never hallucinate. We never let an LLM touch raw numbers. Agents use deterministic data processing tools to produce figures, then report on them. Every claim links back to a specific table or calculation. This is how we achieve reliable insights, whether delivered via chat, documents, or webapps.
business_need:
Our founders have spent decades in market research. Their career frustration has always been that we have wonderful methods to draw valuable insights from data, but rarely the time or budget to deploy them properly. 

Agentic workflows change this equation. They can take the best of our sector and accelerate deployment to a level simply unimaginable before. Teams can try ten different analytical approaches, run advanced segmentation in Python, perform TURF analysis, and see what works. They can produce web-based visualisations to bring the story to life, in minutes. They can work with stakeholders live, doing things in a meeting that would previously have taken weeks. 

Traditionally, data sources were siloed. Now, users can ask a strategic question and let Platinum's agents join the dots across sources. 

Most importantly, Platinum is not a generic AI chat. It learns the context of each project, each team and each organisation, so the   insight it produces is grounded, not generic.
business_potential:
Originality: we believe this is the first system that combines a research-grade tabulation engine, structured-output coding, multi-source input, and agentic workflows behind a single conversational interface, purpose-built for insights. 

Market potential: the global insights industry  (c. $80B+ globally), is under intense margin pressure. Brand insight teams are being asked to deliver more with less whilst maintaining quality. This is the gap Platinum closes. 

Scalability: our stateless cloud architecture requires no per-customer model training. Marginal cost per report approaches the cost of the underlying tokens. We can serve multiple global teams with the same infrastructure, or offer customised on-premise solutions where required. 

Ease of implementation: customers connect existing data, add their organisational context, and are ready to go the same day. No lengthy integration project. No specialist training required. The case study that follows demonstrates this in practice.
case_study:
We introduced Platinum during our presentation at IIEX NA 2026, co-presenting with Sky. Here is a case study of that partnership. 

Sky's insight function faced a common problem: ten research projects across the UK and Ireland, each living in separate systems. Analysis took weeks. When team members moved on, knowledge was lost. 

We deployed Platinum to unify all ten projects into a single platform. The core capabilities included agentic workflows for coding of open-text responses (detecting themes, sentiment and emotion), AI-generated strategic reports with full governance workflow, self-serve dashboards, and early alert systems. Work that previously required weeks of turnaround now takes hours. 

The real test of any platform is whether it can grow beyond its original brief. After the insights team deployment, Sky's Social Media team approached us with their own fragmentation problem: data scattered across TikTok, Facebook, Twitter, Instagram, influencer platforms, PR monitoring tools and internal SharePoint. No single view. No way to connect social signals back to what customers actually said in research. 

Platinum is now being extended to serve this new use case.  5 product dashboards (Brand, Entertainment, Sport, Product, Corporate) plus an aggregated overview, all pulling from the same AI engine. A consistent analytic approach at the organisational level for theming, visualisation and analysis. 

The transformation goes beyond efficiency. Social teams can now validate their observations against structured research data. A spike in negative sentiment on TikTok can be cross-referenced with tracking studies in seconds — separating a genuine shift from noise before the social team has to brief the brand. The platform preserves institutional knowledge, so when people change roles, the insight stays. 

This is Platinum in action: proven delivery in one domain, then horizontal expansion to new teams within the same enterprise. In short, jaw drop moments all round!
