name: Electric Twin
description:
Newton's laws gave science the ability to predict the physical world. There has never been an equivalent for human behaviour, until now. We make predictions about human behaviour work at enterprise scale. Our personas are built from real demographic, cultural and behavioural data, grounded in social science, not prompt engineering. Two products sit at our core: Merlin lets teams deeply understand their audience and generate and test ideas at speed. Echo brings to bear all the information you have on an inaccessible audience so you can simulate your most important conversations. Validation with Prof. Muthukrishna at the LSE puts our models at up to 96% accuracy.
You can ask any question on the platform and receive instant answers. Results come back as quantitative distributions which can be segmented easily. Any respondent group can then be probed with follow-ups for the "why" at an individual or group level.
notes: EU 2026 Rank 2, Score 40.17. Crispin Beale: Electric Twin is an outstanding submission in this cohort by a meaningful margin. It is the only entry that scores at or near the top on every criterion simultaneously: a scientifically grounded and independently validated methodology, a $140B+ marke | Kristi Zuhlke:
how_it_works:
Electric Twin is built on the science of cultural evolution. Beliefs are structured by layers: individual experience, friends and family, online and offline communities, and broader culture. Everyone sits differently across these layers, which is why the same world produces different opinions. It also means opinions are predictable: know where someone sits and you can predict what they think, including things you've never asked.
Real-world seed data captures where someone sits. The LLM provides the landscape of what people in that position believe. A science engine combines them into a grounded response. Brands move from asking their audiences something once a quarter to asking them anything, any time.
Target audience: enterprise insights, brand, marketing and strategy teams in large organisations. 
We go to market as a platform: self-serve, subscription-based. We deliver always-on customer insight that was previously impossible.
business_need:
Three structural problems have impacted the insights industry. Survey response rates have collapsed: Pew's dropped from 36% in 1997 to 6% by 2018, with similar declines across government and academic surveys. This makes panels harder, slower and more expensive to field, and skews results toward the minority still willing to participate. Traditional research also moves too slowly: six-week timelines don't fit business cycles that turn over weekly, so brands run less research than decisions warrant. And audiences that matter most to senior decisions, including executives, policymakers and high-net-worths, have always been hard to reach at scale.
Synthetic audiences unlock a different relationship with research. More and deeper questions, more behavioural insights, cheaper, faster, and across audiences conventional methods can't reach well. Brands move from periodic, static insights to continuous, dynamic and more effective insights.
business_potential:
Most AI audience tools are generic LLMs prompted to roleplay as personas. Electric Twin is structurally different. Our personas are grounded in real demographic, cultural and behavioural data, built on the science of cultural evolution, and validated to 96% accuracy. This is the distinction between simulation and roleplay, and it's what makes the output usable in commercial decisions.
Market potential: global market research is a $140bn+ industry under pressure from collapsing response rates and rising panel costs. Synthetic audiences address the bottleneck and open adjacent markets in stakeholder engagement and policy. We raised $14m from Atomico earlier this year to pursue it.
Scalability: SaaS platform. Marginal cost per simulation is near-zero, revenue scales with additional datasets and seats. News UK, Virgin and BP are customers.
Ease of implementation: browser-based, self-serve.
case_study:
Product in action: a brand insights team tests a new concept against a modelled audience. They design a study, run it against thousands of synthetic respondents in minutes, probe individual respondents for the reasoning behind an answer, then refine and re-run. What used to take six weeks of fieldwork happens in an afternoon.
Deployment: browser-based, self-serve, subscription. Models are configured once against the audience a brand cares about, then used continuously across teams.
Case study: The Times. Over the past year they've integrated Electric Twin across product, marketing and commercial functions. Product teams test design decisions in minutes. Marketing used it for CRM optimisation, copy testing and naming their new business podcast "The Business". Commercial used it to assess audience overlap before committing to partnership deals. The Times validated outputs against a 10% holdout from their 642,000-subscriber base, hitting 92% accuracy versus 93% for traditional panel methods. Result: 10x research volume with the same team.

name: The future of the customer journey
description:
The Future of the Customer Journey explores how AI is reshaping and mediating consumer decision-making. The research focuses on four key objectives: tracking how consumers prompt AI across journey stages; measuring trust, adoption, and decision delegation to AI; analysing AI’s role versus traditional touchpoints; and identifying “AI-enabled” consumer segments driving change in energy use and smart solutions.

For example applied to energy, the study reveals which AI tools consumers use to compare suppliers, tariffs, and bundled solutions (e.g. solar, EVs), how AI supports decisions on pricing, sustainability, and optimisation, and which choices are already delegated (e.g. switching, consumption). It also examines how AI reshapes discovery across channels, influences trust versus brands and peers, impacts loyalty and switching, and creates new segments, highlighting strategic implications for staying visible and trusted as AI intermediates the customer relationship.
notes: EU 2026 Rank 39, Score 28.5. Crispin Beale: The Future of the Customer Journey addresses a genuinely important and underexplored question: as AI mediates more consumer decisions, how do brands understand â€” and remain visible within â€” that mediation layer? how_it_works:
Our solution captures and decodes the emerging AI-mediated customer journey, where decisions are increasingly influenced or executed by AI. We combine syndicated, cross-market research with continuous data collection to track how consumers prompt AI, what they delegate, and how trust shifts across touchpoints. These signals are integrated with attitudinal and behavioral data to model evolving decision pathways and identify “AI-enabled” segments.

This enables brands to move from static, retrospective insights to forward-looking intelligence, understanding not just what consumers choose, but how AI shapes those choices and where influence is shifting.

We target large organizations and insight, strategy, and innovation teams in complex sectors (e.g. energy, durable, beauty, pet food,  telco, finance).

Our syndacated research model is continuous, scalable, faster than ad hoc studies, and able to uncover behaviors and dynamics not visible through traditional methods.
business_need:
A key unmet need in the insights space is understanding how decisions are shaped before they are made, especially as consumers increasingly rely on AI to explore options. Traditional research captures outcomes or stated preferences, but misses the upstream decision logic.

Our solution addresses this by analysing how consumers prompt AI when searching for products or services. These prompts reveal real intent, expectations, and decision criteria in the moment they are formed. This layer is largely invisible to surveys or behavioral data, yet it is where preferences are shaped and choices are influenced.

By decoding prompt behavior at scale, we provide early signals of emerging needs and shifting demand, helping brands understand not just what consumers choose, but how they arrive there in an AI-driven journey.
business_potential:
Our solution meets these criteria by addressing a fundamental shift in how decisions are made in an AI-driven world.

Originality: We move beyond traditional journey mapping by capturing and analysing AI prompts as a new, previously inaccessible layer of consumer behavior. This allows us to decode decision logic in real time, not just outcomes.

Market potential: As AI rapidly becomes a primary interface for search and decision-making across industries, the need to understand its impact is global and cross-sector (energy, finance, telco, retail). This creates strong, scalable demand for forward-looking insights.

Scalability: Built as a syndicated, cross-market model (EU5 and US), the solution is inherently scalable across geographies and categories, with standardized frameworks enabling benchmarking and continuous expansion.
case_study:
For example applied to an energy company’s ecosystem, the study can help address questions such as:
•	which AI tools consumers use when comparing suppliers, tariffs, or bundled solutions (e.g. solar, heat pumps, EV solutions)
•	how AI supports decisions around provider choice, pricing structures, sustainability, and long-term cost optimisation
•	which decisions are already being delegated to AI (e.g. tariff comparison, switching, consumption optimisation, troubleshooting)
•	how AI is reshaping discovery and consideration across direct and indirect channels (comparison sites, aggregators, digital platforms)
•	how consumers prompt AI around energy costs, carbon footprint, reliability, and brand trust
•	the level of trust attributed to AI recommendations versus energy brands, regulators, and peer reviews
•	the impact of AI on customer loyalty, switching behaviour, and engagement
•	the emergence of new consumer segments (e.g. energy optimisers, sustainability-driven users, convenience seekers, sceptics)
•	the strategic implications for remaining visible, trusted, and selected as AI increasingly intermediates customer relationships

name: EXPLNERS
description: One of the biggest issues for client-side insights professionals is getting insights to land with key stakeholders, and getting the attention and the status that they deserve so they can drive change in the business. C-suite professionals don't want to read lengthy, detailed decks, so insight leaders are looking for something more impactful. Video is the best medium for impact and attention - but most of the video used in insight lacks storytelling and is in the form of vox-pop reels, or 'animated PowerPoint'. Our innovation has been to look outside the insight sector and recognise that the explainer video format is perfect for elevating insights. The format tells the story of the insight using animation, cultural references, data visualisation, expert interviews and historical context to make short-form documentaries, put together with wit and stylish presentation. Explainers are widely used in journalism but until we set up EXPLNERS, they were unheard of in the insights sector.

how_it_works: The three co-founders have a unique set of skills. In deliberate contrast to the trend towards automation of outputs, we handcraft our scripts and storyboards using a candid, conversational style that really cuts through compared to typical corporate comms. We have developed a unique house style, which is what our clients find exciting, e.g. we often capture content on film - we re-create charts and data physically and use creative ways to visualise data like using Lego people for family-based segmentation and bagels as donut charts.

Great insights are useless if the key messages don’t land with the stakeholders that need to hear them. Our explainer videos can serve to summarise the key points for people who will never read the deck, or they can serve to publicise the work and drive people to the deck to find out more detail. Either way, they are a powerful tool in the insight activation toolbox. Use cases include debriefs, conferences and key stakeholder meetings such as brand days for retail partners. Our videos make sure that all stakeholders get consistent information in a format they will enjoy, without the insight team having to present over and over again. They create interest and excitement around a project, inspiring audiences and elevating the insight team through clever storytelling and professional production values.
business_potential:
Originality: many firms make explainer videos, but no others exclusively make videos to explain market research. The fit of the format to our sector is so neat that you wonder why nobody thought of it before.
Potential: as research becomes democratised and DIY, there is even more need for skilled researchers who can take a creative approach to explaining what it all means. The elephant in the room is AI video, but what we do has too many moving parts to make with a prompt: understanding the insights, knowing what will be of value to stakeholders, finding the story and building a creative approach.
Scalability: We’ve seen strong income, project size and margin growth since launch. We’re not an infinitely scalable platform; we scale by building a network of creative specialists - providing employment in our sector. The future? Increase the team and continue to provide a premium hand-made creative product.
Ease: we think we’re a delight to work with. Fortunately, clients seem to agree.
case_study: We work directly with brands or through agency partners. We are often engaged at the end of a project when there is a full report to work from, but can also be brought in after a project has been delivered if it hasn’t got the attention it needs, or when there is a particular event coming up such as a workshop, conference or presentation that needs a WOW moment. Our process is as follows: immerse in the data plus desk research around the topic and brand; interview key stakeholders if required; find the most compelling story in the data and develop a narrative structure; write a voiceover if required; build a storyboard with audiovisual direction; film any content we need; select any client footage to use; produce the video. Our videos range from 90s shorts to five minute plus full explainers, and we can employ any look, feel and tone, from slick and professional, to dramatic and impactful, to charming and humorous. We work with a wide range of clients, from FMCG giants such as PepsiCo, Unilever and McCain, specialist producers such as Lakeland Dairies and Specialized Bikes and public sector organisations such as the ICO. A CPG brand runs a monthly brand tracker survey (n=500) via Alchemer. Once fieldwork closes, their insights manager triggers the Insight Genius workflow through a single Claude conversation:

Step 1 — Open-end coding: The .sav SPSS file is uploaded to Talk2Data. The manager asks "Code Q12 (brand perception open end) into 8 categories and show me frequency by segment." The MCP processes the file and returns a coded dataset with a summary table in under 3 minutes.

Step 2 — Statistical analysis: Still in the same conversation, she asks "Which segments show the biggest decline vs last month in brand trust?" Talk2Data runs the crosstab comparison and flags two segments: Males 18-24 and Southern region — both down 6 points.

Step 3 — Narrative generation: She pastes the output into a Gamma prompt and generates a 10-slide presentation with the key findings, charts, and a recommended action: adjust digital creative messaging targeting Males 18-24 on social.

Step 4 — Ad copy update: The insight feeds directly into their performance marketing team, who update Meta ad copy that same afternoon — replacing generic brand messaging with copy addressing the specific trust gap identified.

Total time from data file to actionable brief: under 90 minutes. Previously: 2–3 days.

name: Concept Iteration & Response Calibration Assistant (CIRCA)
description: CIRCA (Concept Iteration & Response Calibration Assistant) is an AI-powered concept generation tool that transforms consumer feedback into high-quality visual outputs in real time. Unlike traditional research, which is slow, linear, and largely verbal, CIRCA enables rapid, iterative co-creation by instantly translating feedback into refined product concepts, moodboards, or storyboards within the same session. Its key innovation lies in preserving core design elements while selectively evolving based on feedback, ensuring structured progression rather than random generation. By seamlessly moving from product to concept to communication, it significantly reduces creative testing timelines, enhances insight clarity, and supports multilingual inputs, enabling faster, more aligned decision-making across diverse consumer contexts.
notes: EU 2026 Rank 16, Score 33.83. Crispin Beale: CIRCA is a well-evidenced submission with one of the most credible named clients in the cohort. Colgate-Palmolive running ongoing NPD and innovation work across global oral care categories, with a named Innovation Insights Director willing to be cite | Kristi Zuhlke:
how_it_works:

A base concept is defined, after which consumer feedback is captured live and translated into structured fine-tune inputs. The system uses AI to generate refined visual outputs—product concepts, moodboards, or storyboards—while preserving core design elements and evolving selectively based on feedback. Each output becomes the input for the next cycle, enabling continuous refinement.

This shifts research from static evaluation to dynamic co-creation, allowing consumers to react to visuals rather than abstract ideas, improving clarity of attitudes and preferences. Designed for research teams, agencies, and brands, CIRCA operates as a fast, scalable platform, reducing iteration cycles from weeks to minutes and enabling deeper, more actionable insights beyond conventional methods.

name: Priceagent - Prediction Demand Engine
description:
Most market research platforms tell brands what consumers want. None answer the question that drives revenue most directly: how much they will pay. Priceagent fills this gap with the Predictive Demand Engine (PDE), a proprietary algorithm that maps consumer willingness to pay (WTP) as a continuous demand curve, not a static data point.
Where legacy methods deliver vague price zones, the PDE delivers exact coordinates: the precise price that maximizes demand, the precise price that maximizes revenue, and the psychological barriers in between that most companies never see.
Studies run in hours to days, not weeks to months. Results are visual, intuitive, and immediately actionable, no analyst needed. Any product, marketing, or leadership team can go from market question to decision in days - even as fast as an hour.
notes: EU 2026 Rank 3, Score 39.33. Crispin Beale: Priceagent is one of my highest scoring submissions in the cohort. It earns that position through a rare combination: a genuinely original technical approach, a near-universal market opportunity, exceptional presentation quality, and a named Tier 1 c | Kristi Zuhlke:
how_it_works: A brand describes their product. Priceagent's AI builds a contextual consumer survey, localized in language and currency, and deploys it to a targeted panel. Respondents first configure their ideal version of the product, psychologically anchoring their willingness to pay to a real purchase decision rather than an abstract guess. The Predictive Demand Engine processes each response not as a single data point, but as a continuous range of viable price points, accumulated across all respondents to calculate demand and revenue at every price coordinate. The output delivers exact price points, not zones: including the price that maximizes demand, the price that maximizes revenue, psychological Price Walls and Plateaus, and AI-driven Price Pulse narratives translating the curve into plain strategic recommendations. Teams can further segment results by product attributes and demographics, revealing exactly which features drive willingness to pay, and which don't.
business_need:
The global insights industry excels at understanding what consumers want, but has no reliable, scalable way to answer how much they will pay:
>Legacy methods are slow and expensive: weeks-long research requiring specialist analysts, putting pricing intelligence out of reach for most teams
>No exact price points: existing methods deliver zones or interpolated estimates, never the precise price that maximizes revenue
>Segmentation is an afterthought: tools rarely connect product attribute preferences directly to willingness to pay
>Pricing is a one-time decision:teams lack on-demand tools to track how WTP shifts across the full product lifecycle
>Product development lacks pricing input: features are built without knowing what consumers actually value enough to pay for
>Bundling is guesswork: no consumer-backed data to determine what belongs in basic, mid, or premium tiers
business_potential:
Every company in the world prices something. Yet pricing decisions, one of the highest-leverage choices a business makes, are still routinely made on gut feeling, competitor benchmarking, or outdated cost-plus models. Priceagent addresses a near-universal business need.
>Originality: no existing SaaS platform combines continuous-range WTP modeling, contextual survey design, AI localization, and segmentation analysis in a single self-serve tool
>Market potential: any company launching a product, entering a new market, adjusting for cost shocks, or optimizing promotions is a potential customer, across every industry and geography
>Scalability: fully automated, AI-driven, and deployable across languages, currencies and markets without human intervention. One platform, global reach
>Ease of implementation: no integration, no analyst, no agency. A team can sign up, run their first study and have actionable results the same day.
case_study:
Priceagent is already being used by global brands to make high-stakes pricing decisions with confidence. Here is one recent example.
Client: Edrington, global premium spirits brand (e.g. The Macallan, Highland Park, Grey Goose)
Challenge: In Sweden, Systembolaget holds a retail monopoly on all spirits. Without traditional retail levers like promotions or shelf placement, pricing and positioning are the only tools available to win market share. Edrington needed to understand what Swedish whisky consumers were actually willing to pay for, not just what they preferred.
What Priceagent did: Edrington used the Priceagent platform to run a willingness-to-pay study across their portfolio. The PDE mapped consumer demand across price levels and identified which specific product attributes Swedish consumers were genuinely prepared to pay a premium for versus attributes they wanted but wouldn't pay more to have.
Outcome: The insights led to a strategic recalibration of Edrington's portfolio, including the exact price point at which including a premium gift box added measurable demand versus where it did not justify the cost. The team adjusted brand positioning across price tiers, packaging investment priorities, and value driver alignment across the Systembolaget channel.
"What we gained was a much clearer understanding of customers' willingness to pay and how that should guide our commercial decisions." - Rozbeh Saberian, Edrington

name: QuestionPunk
description:
Two AI breakthroughs reshaped research. Generative AI made concepts free; AI moderators made feedback fast (Listen Labs, Outset, NewtonX).
Incumbents are now connecting these halves into autonomous loops — but with synthetic respondents (Zappi, Kantar) supplying the preference signal. AI scores AI. Fast, but bounded by training data, not what real consumers in your priority segment will do.

QuestionPunk is built around a sharp split: synthetic AI where it scales — generation, coding, orchestration — and real respondents where it counts: the preference signal that decides what ships. Describe a problem; the system generates concepts, fields them to a real-respondent panel, reads what won with which demographic, generates the next round biased toward winners, and re-fields. Synthetic is scaffolding. Real humans are load-bearing.
Iteration happens at AI speed. The signal stays human.
notes: EU 2026 Rank 6, Score 36.83. Crispin Beale: QuestionPunk has the sharpest competitive landscape analysis and some of the best writing in the cohort. The synthetic/real split is architecturally precise and correctly identifies the gap between two established categories. The market sizing is pro | Kristi Zuhlke:
how_it_works:
A brand manager: "Find the snack packaging Gen Z women will actually buy."

Generation. Friday evening: a draft survey and a set of seed concepts spanning the design space. AI personas pre-flight the instrument, flagging confusing questions and demographic gaps.

Feedback. Saturday: real respondents take the study by voice or web. The AI moderator probes surprising answers, skips the obvious. Transcripts, themes, and rubric scores populate live.

Iteration. Sunday: an optimizer generates round two weighted toward what won with each demographic segment and re-fields automatically.

Decision. Monday: a winning concept — and a different winner for her priority Latina Gen Z subset.

Audience: brand insights teams, agencies, academic researchers, innovation teams.

Disruption: competitors are closed enterprise services priced for procurement. QuestionPunk is self-serve SaaS — a researcher fields a study within the hour and pays per study.
business_need:
Research has two costly bottlenecks: generating concepts ($30K creative rounds) and getting feedback ($50K engagements). Studies took months — both halves human-bottlenecked.

Two years collapsed each cost. Generative AI made concepts unlimited; AI moderation made feedback fast. Each side got 100× faster.

But each side stops at its boundary. A human picks six concepts, uploads them, reads results, decides what to refine. The bottleneck moved; it didn't disappear. Research is throttled by the human in between the AI tools.

Incumbents are now closing that loop with synthetic respondents (Zappi, Kantar) — fast, but bounded by AI training data and short of what real consumers in your priority segment will do. Concept testing's job is to predict real-world response. A loop that never touches a real consumer can't.

The unmet need: autonomous iteration with real human signal, segmented by demographic. No platform combines all three. QuestionPunk does.
business_potential:
Originality. Synthetic-respondent loops (Zappi, Kantar) are bounded by AI training data — AI scores AI. Real-respondent platforms (Listen Labs, Outset, NewtonX, Quantilope) preserve human signal but need a human to drive iteration. QuestionPunk splits the two: synthetic for generation, coding, and orchestration; real respondents for the preference signal that decides what ships. The only platform built to close the loop autonomously with real ground-truth signal, segmented by demographic.

Market potential. The global insights industry is ~$140B (ESOMAR 2024). Concept testing alone is $5.6B today, projected to $12B by 2032 at 8.7% CAGR.

Scalability. Pure software. Marginal cost approaches API + panel pass-through. Same codebase serves a graduate student and an enterprise CPG team.

Ease of implementation. No SDK, no procurement, no RFP. A researcher fields a study within the hour. Studies that took six weeks and $80K take three days at a fraction of the cost.
case_study:
A regional CPG company is launching a better-for-you snack line targeting Gen Z women. Traditional approach: hire a design agency ($60K, six weeks) for six concepts, run a focus group ($25K, three weeks), pick a winner. Total: ~$85K and nine weeks before any real consumer signal.

On QuestionPunk, the brand manager states the goal Friday afternoon. By Saturday she has dozens of packaging variants generated on the platform and fielded to 500 demographically-matched real respondents (via external panel integration), each scoring appeal and purchase intent with voice-recorded rationales. Sunday, the system generates a second round weighted toward what won with each demographic segment and re-fields automatically. Monday morning she has a winning concept, a different winner for her priority Latina Gen Z subset, and the verbatim "why" behind each segment. Time elapsed: 60 hours.

name: Socialtrait
description:
Surveys ask people what they think. Reality asks what they do. The gap between the two is where billions in marketing spend disappears.
Socialtrait closes that gap. We've built the predictive infrastructure layer for human behaviour — an enterprise simulation platform where over a million AI agents form opinions, hold memory, influence each other, and react inside a persistent social fabric calibrated to a brand's real audience.
What makes us different: we don't prompt a chatbot to "act like a 35-year-old mum from Texas." That gives you something coherent and rational. It cannot give you the messiness — the contradictions, the irrationality, the chaos where real human decisions actually live.
Our patented Agent–World Protocol is the universal interface between mind and world: one persistent persona moves across surveys, focus groups, social simulations, and AI customer-experience tests, retaining identity throughout. Output: insights in hours, validated at 90% accuracy to ground truth.
notes: EU 2026 Rank 5, Score 38.83. Crispin Beale: Socialtrait is one of the most commercially complete submissions in the cohort and scores highly. A named case study would help. business_need:
The insights industry is wrestling with three structural breaks at once.
The say–do gap is the oldest: stated-preference research predicts what people say, not what they do. At IIEX Europe 2025, 40.6% of insights leaders named this their hardest unsolved problem.
The second is the AI-bot contamination of survey panels — recall bias and bot fraud are quietly collapsing the reliability of the very instruments brands depend on. Paying members were getting less and waiting longer for it. And the year-long $1,000 qualification threshold was inverted into a $500-in-3-months sprint, unlocking a guest segment the brand had been trying to reach for years. name: Purify Intelligence
description:
The Purify Intelligence platform transforms survey data from legacy formats into structured, analytics-ready data in real time, making it instantly usable across modern BI tools like Tableau as well as AI and agentic systems. 

This matters because insight has always been grounded in facts, but AI is changing how those facts are consumed. AI does not fix bad data, it amplifies it. Poorly structured survey data leads to misinterpretation, weak outputs and loss of trust. 

There is a common misconception that AI needs to be trained on survey data. In reality, it needs to reference it. Purify makes data consistently structured, transparent and machine-readable, so AI can accurately query and interpret it without retraining or duplication. 

What was once a slow, manual transformation process becomes automated and scalable, allowing organisations to connect survey data into wider data ecosystems, BI platforms and AI workflows without compromising the integrity of their insight.
notes: EU 2026 Rank 38, Score 28.83. Crispin Beale: Lacks evidence. Purify Intelligence addresses a real and underserved infrastructure problem â€” survey data locked in SPSS files while enterprises invest in Snowflake and Databricks â€” with a technically sound architectural approach and the most pre | Kristi Zuhlke: no case study
how_it_works:
The Purify Intelligence platform works with modern medallion architecture principles, similar to Databricks or Snowflake. The first level is to upload survey data, either via an API to a survey platform such as Decipher, or via a csv or SPSS upload. From there, the platform automates a series of data transformations, including automated cleaning, coding, text formatting, and other functions typically run via a data processing team. Once completed the platform then converts the data files into a big data format, where it can be pushed into a data lake of choice for the client, where it is then ready to be retrieved and used for dashboarding or Agentic AI. 

Our target audiences are both agencies wishing to deliver data and insights to their clients in a faster, more streamlined and connected way, and also end clients themselves who have data analytics and engineering teams that would like to incorporate primary survey data into their methodologies but are currently unable to do so.
business_need:
The insights industry faces a fundamental inefficiency on how survey data is processed, stored, and delivered. Data is typically locked in files which require manual processing, tabulating, and reporting. This creates delays, manual processes, and frustrations from clients who expect data to be delivered fast and accurately. As a result, survey data is often seen as being outside the core analytical workflows and too complex to integrate with the tools businesses actually use to make decisions. 

In parallel, end-clients are investing into platforms such as Snowflake, Databricks or Fabric. Yet these platforms rely on well-structured, accessible data which traditional research outputs do not provide.  

Purify Intelligence addresses this gap by transforming survey data into structured, interoperable formats. Enabling agencies to move beyond static deliverables and integrate insight data directly into modern analytics ecosystems, placing it back at the centre of business decision-making.
business_potential:
There is nothing else on the market that can automate the transformation and delivery of survey data in the way that Purify Intelligence can. The only alternative is an expensive data engineering team that would manually process data files on a case-by-case basis.

Our goal is to become the Databricks of the market research industry, bringing survey data into the modern data world.
The platform is built on medallion architecture principles, giving it a modular and extensible foundation. Rather than being a fixed solution, it is designed to evolve. New capabilities can be added without reworking the core system, whether that is additional data sources, transformation logic, outputs, or integrations.
This includes seamless integration with modern BI tools and AI agents, where structured, well-prepared data can be referenced directly rather than requiring retraining. As new standards and approaches emerge, such as the Open Semantic Interchange, the platform is already positioned to adapt.

These are now simple queries, not manual searches. Teams reuse previous work, reduce duplication, and answer new questions in minutes rather than days. Crucially, respondent-level data is no longer locked in files. It is retained in a structured, governed format, enabling secure, permissioned analysis without the need to extract or rebuild datasets. In this scenario, research shifts from static deliverables to a connected, continuously updating data asset. The more studies ABC conducts, the more valuable their data becomes, turning a decade of research into a compounding asset rather than a growing archive.

For the first time, all media audiences - including in-home but also commuters, device sharers, screen stackers, fan park viewers, audiences with no internet access and even people with no mobile devices at all - can be measured & researched using a single restech platform.
Developed in & for emerging markets, Mediamate combines wearable technology, intelligent passive audio detection, Bluetooth monitoring & panelist interaction into one versatile, efficient media research tool. Our vision, shared with our pilot partners and investors, is to make cutting edge tech-driven media research and measurement accessible across the world, not just in developed markets with advanced infrastructure and massive budgets.

how_it_works:
The heart of Mediamate is a wearable device that collects 4 data streams - three "passive" & one "active":
- Intelligent monitoring & detection of audible media content in the wearer's immediate soundscape
- Bluetooth monitoring of digital media activity on the wearer's devices
- Biometric sensing of wearer's pulse rate & temperature
- A chat portal for "active" quantitative & qualitative research
Because they all tap into the audience experience of the same person, these streams can be combined in many valuable ways, for example combining passive reach measurement with active impact evaluation for a media campaign. Built-in 4G connectivity provides independence from the panelist's own internet connection (or lack thereof), & an embedded agentic AI ensures the device operates normally if the 4G connection is lost.
A management portal allows us to monitor device activity, programme detection schedules, upload content for identification & design and distribute research tasks.
business_need:
Billions of media interactions go unmeasured and unresearched every day because they happen in contexts that are too difficult and/or expensive to collect data from. Unresearched media interactions don't move the needle for advertisers and investors, they don't grow the industry and they don't advance the needs of the audience.
A big part of the problem is that the available data collection tools are typically developed in and for audiences in developed markets, and require a certain level of digital infrastructure to operate, such as a home wifi network and/or a smartphone. So audiences with limited digital infrastructure (at least half the population of Africa, for example) are often underresearched, undermeasured, or ignored completely. Mediamate bridges that gap, with profound implications for global media research inclusivity and harnessing the massive economic power of the developing world.
business_potential: The system can be rolled out at any scale, anywhere in the world. For example, one of our pilot partners plans to deploy Mediamates to panels in Ghana to monitor the reach and effectiveness of educational radio programming that helps farmers adapt to climate change. In South Africa, another partner is setting up an OOH sports viewer panel to measure and understand media consumption in sports bars, fan parks and other public places, a complete blind spot currently. This is just the tip of the iceberg, we are also in discussions to roll out Mediamate panels in over a dozen other countries.
case_study:
Mentioned a couple of case studies in earlier sections, others that we're exploring with partners include:
- using the biometric sensor to measure pulse rate before/during/after exposure to specific media content, to explore emotional engagement
- Going beyond ATL media by detecting attendance at events, activations, roadshows etc and exploring with primary research
- Measuring and researching screenstacking activity, for example the use of betting apps while watching sports (in home AND out of home)
- Reinforcing confidence in online survey data - we know the respondent is not a bot but a real human with a pulse, because we're monitoring it.
